{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_0fe3d16cea84414e94e5f87f","type":"tool_result","content":"3266\t\n3267\t**Capabilities.**\n3268\t- **Instant-search** with 150 ms debounce; uses §13.10 query coalescing so concurrent keystrokes across users collapse to a single scatter\n3269\t- **Combined round-trip**: results + all facet counts fetched in a single `/multi-search` (§13.11) call per keystroke\n3270\t- **URL state**: every query + filter + sort + page is encoded in the URL so results are bookmarkable and shareable (`?q=laptop&brand=apple&price=500-2000&sort=price:asc&page=2`)\n3271\t- **Keyboard navigation**: `/` to focus the search box, arrow keys to move through results, `Enter` to open, `Esc` to clear\n3272\t- **Highlighting** of matched terms in title and snippet (uses Meilisearch's native `_formatted` output)\n3273\t- **Typo tolerance UI** with \"did you mean\" suggestions surfaced naturally when zero hits are returned\n3274\t- **Empty state** with popular queries (seeded from §13.18 canaries or operator-defined suggestions)\n3275\t- **Error states** with friendly, non-technical messaging and a \"report a problem\" link\n3276\t- **Pagination** — infinite scroll on mobile, classic pagination on desktop (configurable)\n3277\t- **Dark mode** via `prefers-color-scheme` and a manual toggle stored in `localStorage`\n3278\t- **Internationalization** — bundled with English; operator-supplied locale JSONs load on demand via `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/{lang}.json`\n3279\t\n3280\t**Design philosophy (beautiful and functional, desktop + mobile).**\n3281\t\n3282\t- **Aesthetic.** Content-first: the UI recedes, the results come forward. Generous whitespace. Strong typographic hierarchy — one variable-weight sans-serif (system-ui → Inter → sans-serif). Subtle motion design: 180 ms fade + translate on result-card enter; no gratuitous animation. Rounded card corners (12 px), soft shadows (1–3 px blur), thin dividers. Single configurable accent color drives CTAs and highlights; the rest is a tight neutral grayscale. Imagery supported with graceful fallbacks — missing thumbnails degrade to an elegant placeholder with the first letter of the title.\n3283\t- **Responsive breakpoints.**\n3284\t  - **Mobile (< 640 px)** — single column; sticky search header; facets hidden behind a bottom-sheet drawer with a \"Filter\" button; result cards full-width with a 3:2 thumbnail on top; infinite scroll; touch targets ≥ 44 × 44 px; pull-to-refresh; back-gesture aware (browser back clears filters, not navigates away).\n3285\t  - **Tablet (640–1024 px)** — two-column: collapsible facets rail on the left, results on the right; card grid of 2 columns; toggle between card and list view.\n3286\t  - **Desktop (≥ 1024 px)** — three-column option: facets / results / optional preview pane; card grid of 3 or 4 columns; hover-preview on cards; keyboard shortcuts visible in footer; dense mode available.\n3287\t  - **Large desktop (≥ 1440 px)** — max content width clamp at 1440 px centered, preventing awkward stretched layouts on ultra-wide monitors.\n3288\t- **Accessibility.** WCAG 2.2 AA end-to-end: semantic HTML landmarks (`<search>`, `<main>`, `<nav>`); ARIA live region announces result counts after each query; all facets are labeled and reachable via Tab; focus rings always visible; color-blind-safe accent defaults; `prefers-reduced-motion` disables all motion; screen-reader tested flows for search, filter, and open-result.\n3289\t- **Performance.**\n3290\t  - Total initial payload ≤ 60 KB gzipped (HTML + JS + CSS + fonts)\n3291\t  - **Preact** (not React) + vanilla CSS custom properties\n3292\t  - No client-side bundler runtime; a single pre-built module\n3293\t  - System fonts default; custom web fonts loaded with `font-display: swap` and only if explicitly configured\n3294\t  - Images lazy-loaded with `loading=\"lazy\"` and `decoding=\"async\"`\n3295\t  - Layout-shift-free skeleton loaders during instant-search keystrokes\n3296\t  - Mobile network target: Lighthouse performance ≥ 95 on a mid-tier Android over 4G\n3297\t  - SSR-free — fully static SPA, cache-able at the edge\n3298\t- **Input modalities.** Touch on mobile (tap to select facet, tap card to open, pull-to-refresh), mouse on desktop (hover, right-click context menus optional), keyboard full coverage (`/`, arrows, Enter, Esc, `g f` to toggle filters).\n3299\t\n3300\t**Embeddable modes.**\n3301\t- **Iframe** — `<iframe src=\"https://search.example.com/ui/search/products?embed=true\">` strips chrome and adapts to container width; `postMessage` events for height auto-resize and result-clicked.\n3302\t- **Web component** — `<script src=\"https://search.example.com/ui/widget.js\"></script>` + `<miroir-search index=\"products\" accent=\"#2563eb\"></miroir-search>` drops a fully-featured search into any page.\n3303\t- **Headless mode** — `?headless=true` returns only the results container, no search input or facets, for use as part of a larger custom UI.\n3304\t\n3305\t**Custom templates.** `result_template: custom` allows operators to POST an HTML template with `{{field}}` / `{{#if}}` Handlebars-style interpolation. Templates are stored in the task store and served to the SPA at runtime. Template errors are caught and logged; the UI falls back to the default card template rather than breaking.\n3306\t\n3307\t**Security.**\n3308\t- Content-Security-Policy restricts script and style origins\n3309\t- All document fields are HTML-escaped before rendering (XSS-safe)\n3310\t- Rate limiting per source IP (configurable; default 60 rpm)\n3311\t- The SPA holds only a short-lived JWT session token; the search-scoped Meilisearch key is orchestrator-held and rotated (see \"Authentication model\" above) — never the master key, never the node key, never embedded in HTML\n3312\t- CORS configurable per deployment (see `search_ui.cors_allowed_origins` and `admin_ui.cors_allowed_origins` below)\n3313\t\n3314\t**Analytics (idempotent click-throughs).** When `search_ui.analytics.enabled: true`, the SPA emits a beacon on result click and on search-completion for end-user latency tracking via `POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/beacon` (see §4 Admin API table). Every click-through beacon carries an `event_id: <client-generated uuid>` in the body (§13.10 idempotency cache uses this field directly; the `Idempotency-Key` header is not required because the endpoint uses `event_id` as its dedup key); the browser synthesizes the UUID once per unique `(query, result_id, session)` tuple for click-throughs and `(session, minute_bucket)` for latency beacons, and reuses it on any retry — page refreshes that replay the same click from history do not double-count. The emitted CDC event (`type: click_through` or `type: latency`) uses `event_id` as its identity, and downstream consumers dedupe on that field. Latency events are subject to `cdc.emit_internal_writes` — operators who do not want end-user latency noise in their CDC stream can leave that flag at its default to suppress them. If the SPA cannot generate UUIDs (very old browsers without `crypto.randomUUID`), the orchestrator falls back to `hash(session_id || query || result_id || minute_bucket)` server-side. Event shape published to CDC:\n3315\t\n3316\t```json\n3317\t{\n3318\t  \"type\": \"click_through\",\n3319\t  \"event_id\": \"<idempotency key>\",\n3320\t  \"session_id\": \"<opaque session id>\",\n3321\t  \"query\": \"<query string>\",\n3322\t  \"result_id\": \"<primary key of clicked hit>\",\n3323\t  \"result_position\": 3,\n3324\t  \"index\": \"products\",\n3325\t  \"timestamp\": 1712345678901\n3326\t}\n3327\t```\n3328\t\n3329\t**Compatibility.** Purely orchestrator-served. All data flows through the existing search API. Uses §13.11 multi-search for results + facets; §13.6 session pinning for read-your-writes if the host app is doing concurrent writes; §13.10 query coalescing for efficient instant-search.\n3330\t\n3331\t**Config.**\n3332\t```yaml\n3333\tsearch_ui:\n3334\t  enabled: true\n3335\t  path: /ui/search\n3336\t  widget_script_enabled: true\n3337\t  embeddable: true\n3338\t\n3339\t  auth:\n3340\t    mode: public                    # public | shared_key | oauth_proxy\n3341\t    shared_key_env: \"\"              # env var holding the shared key (shared_key mode)\n3342\t    session_ttl_s: 900\n3343\t    session_rate_limit: \"10/minute\"\n3344\t    jwt_secret_env: SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET\n3345\t    oauth_proxy:\n3346\t      user_header: X-Forwarded-User\n3347\t      groups_header: X-Forwarded-Groups\n3348\t      filter_template: \"tenant IN [{groups}]\"   # null = disable filter injection\n3349\t      attribute_map:                            # optional per-claim binding\n3350\t        groups: groups_array\n3351\t        user: user_id_string\n3352\t\n3353\t  allowed_origins: [\"*\"]\n3354\t  scoped_key_max_age_days: 60                  # hard expiry on the scoped Meilisearch key (upper bound on key lifetime)\n3355\t  scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days: 30     # rotate this many days before the key's expiry (must be strictly less than scoped_key_max_age_days; see \"Config validation\" below)\n3356\t  scoped_key_rotation_drain_s: 120     # wait this long after the new generation is in place before revoking the previous key; see \"Revocation safety gate\" above\n3357\t\n3358\t  rate_limit:\n3359\t    per_ip: \"60/minute\"\n3360\t    backend: redis                  # redis | local (per-pod); redis required when replicas > 1\n3361\t    redis_key_prefix: \"miroir:ratelimit:searchui:\"\n3362\t    redis_ttl_s: 60\n3363\t\n3364\t  cors_allowed_origins: []          # empty = same-origin only\n3365\t  csp_overrides:\n3366\t    script_src: []                  # additional script-src domains\n3367\t    img_src: []                     # additional img-src domains\n3368\t    connect_src: []                 # additional connect-src for XHR targets\n3369\t  csp: \"default-src 'self'; img-src 'self' https:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'\"   # base template; any csp_overrides.* lists are merged into the corresponding directives at render time\n3370\t\n3371\t  analytics:\n3372\t    enabled: false\n3373\t    sink: cdc                     # publishes click-throughs as CDC events\n3374\t```\n3375\t\n3376\tWith `rate_limit.backend: local` and multi-pod deployments, the effective per-IP rate is `per_ip × pod_count` per minute cluster-wide (each pod maintains its own bucket table). For production deployments with `miroir.replicas > 1`, the Helm chart's `values.schema.json` rejects `backend: local` at install time, forcing Redis-backed shared state. The Redis bucket keys live under `redis_key_prefix` with `redis_ttl_s` TTL so memory stays bounded even under scan/spray attacks.\n3377\t\n3378\t**Filter injection in `oauth_proxy` mode.** When `search_ui.auth.oauth_proxy.filter_template` is non-null, Miroir automatically injects the rendered filter into every `/indexes/{uid}/search` call made under the session, using Meilisearch filter-concatenation semantics (the rendered clause is ANDed with any user-supplied filter the SPA sends — `(<user filter>) AND (<injected filter>)`). The `{groups}` placeholder is replaced at session-mint time with the comma-joined values of the `groups_header` (properly JSON-array-escaped for the Meilisearch filter DSL, e.g. `[\"engineering\",\"ops\"]`); the rendered claim is baked into the JWT so every subsequent search under that session enforces the same filter without re-reading proxy headers. `{user}` is replaced with the `user_header` value. `attribute_map` lets operators rename the JWT claim keys exposed to observability tooling and to the client (read-only — the SPA cannot override them). When `filter_template` is null, Miroir performs no filter injection — operators relying on a reverse proxy or downstream filter-at-app layer for access control must enforce it themselves; Miroir still forwards the user/group claims as read-only JWT claims for observability but does not gate results on them.\n3379\t\n3380\t**Metrics.** `miroir_search_ui_sessions_total`, `miroir_search_ui_queries_total{index}`, `miroir_search_ui_zero_hits_total{index}`, `miroir_search_ui_click_through_total{index}` (when analytics enabled), `miroir_search_ui_p95_ms{index}` (end-user-observed latency, measured via client beacon).\n3381\t\n3382\t---\n3383\t\n3384\t## 14. Resource Envelope and Horizontal Scaling\n3385\t\n3386\tMiroir is designed to fit within a fixed per-pod resource envelope. If aggregate workload exceeds what one pod can serve, Miroir scales **out**, not up — the orchestrator is stateless on the request path and partitions background work across pods by explicit design.\n3387\t\n3388\t### 14.1 Target envelope\n3389\t\n3390\tEach orchestrator pod is sized for:\n3391\t\n3392\t- **2 vCPU**\n3393\t- **3.75 GB RAM**\n3394\t\n3395\tThis matches common small-instance tiers (AWS t3.medium, GCP e2-medium, Hetzner CX22, Rackspace Spot 2c/3.75GB). Meilisearch nodes are sized independently per Section 6 and are unaffected by this envelope.\n3396\t\n3397\t### 14.2 Per-pod memory budget\n3398\t\n3399\tSteady-state allocation at default config, assuming all advanced capabilities enabled:\n3400\t\n3401\t| Component | Budget | Primary knob |\n3402\t|-----------|--------|--------------|\n3403\t| Rust/Tokio runtime + axum baseline | 80 MB | — |\n3404\t| HTTP/2 connection pools to nodes | 50 MB | `connection_pool_per_node` |\n3405\t| Request/response buffers (p99 concurrent) | 200 MB | `server.max_body_bytes`, `max_concurrent_requests` |\n3406\t| Task registry cache | 100 MB | `task_registry.cache_size` |\n3407\t| Idempotency cache (§13.10) | 100 MB | `idempotency.max_cached_keys` |\n3408\t| Session pinning state (§13.6) | 50 MB | `session_pinning.max_sessions` |\n3409\t| Query coalescing in-flight (§13.10) | 50 MB | `query_coalescing.max_subscribers` |\n3410\t| Router + EWMA state (§13.3) | 20 MB | fixed per node |\n3411\t| Query plan cache (§13.4) | 20 MB | fixed |\n3412\t| Alias table (§13.7) | 10 MB | fixed |\n3413\t| Metrics registry (prometheus) | 50 MB | fixed |\n3414\t| Dump import buffer (§13.9, only while importing) | 128 MB | `dump_import.memory_buffer_bytes` |\n3415\t| Anti-entropy fingerprinter (§13.8, only during pass) | 128 MB | `anti_entropy.max_read_concurrency` × batch |\n3416\t| Multi-search batch state (§13.11) | ~5 MB | `multi_search.max_queries_per_batch` (per-request cache) |\n3417\t| Vector search over-fetch scratch (§13.12) | ~30 MB | `vector_search.over_fetch_factor` (larger result buffers during merge) |\n3418\t| CDC in-memory batch buffer (§13.13, only when CDC enabled) | 64 MB | `cdc.buffer.memory_bytes` |\n3419\t| TTL sweeper state (§13.14) | ~5 MB | cursor per owned shard (negligible) |\n3420\t| Tenant map LRU (§13.15, only when `mode: api_key`) | ~20 MB | `tenant_affinity.mode` |\n3421\t| Shadow tee in-flight duplicates (§13.16) | varies (~50 MB worst case at 5% sample) | `shadow.targets[].sample_rate` × avg_response_size × concurrency |\n3422\t| Rollover policy evaluator (§13.17) | negligible | fixed |\n3423\t| Canary runner state (§13.18) | ~20 MB | `canary_runner.run_history_per_canary` (golden responses + run history LRU) |\n3424\t| Admin UI embedded assets (§13.19) | ~10 MB | fixed (rust-embed compressed + decompressed working set) |\n3425\t| Explain plan cache (§13.20) | ~10 MB | fixed |\n3426\t| Search UI embedded assets (§13.21) | ~10 MB | fixed |\n3427\t| Search UI rate-limiter state (§13.21) | ~20 MB (Redis-backed when `replicas > 1`) | `search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip` / `search_ui.rate_limit.backend` (IP buckets; see §13.21 rate-limiter shared-state note and §14.7 Redis memory accounting) |\n3428\t| Allocator overhead + headroom for bursts | 800 MB | — |\n3429\t| **Steady-state total (idle background)** | **~1.2 GB** | |\n3430\t| **Total with one heavy background job active** | **~1.7 GB** | |\n3431\t\n3432\tWith all new §13.11–21 features enabled simultaneously, steady-state budget rises by roughly **200 MB** versus the pre-§13.11 baseline, staying within the 3.75 GB envelope but tightening the burst headroom. Operators can disable individual capabilities (see §13 preamble — every `enabled: true` knob can be flipped off) to reclaim memory on constrained deployments. Remaining ~2 GB of the 3.75 GB envelope absorbs request concurrency spikes and GC/allocator behavior. Heavy background jobs (dump import, reshard backfill) only run on pods that have claimed them from the shared queue — not every pod simultaneously — so total budget stays within envelope even cluster-wide.\n3433\t\n3434\t### 14.3 Per-pod CPU budget\n3435\t\n3436\t| Path | Cost | Scaling |\n3437\t|------|------|---------|\n3438\t| Scatter fan-out dispatch | ~5% of 1 core per 1 kQPS | Linear in RG × RF |\n3439\t| JSON parse + re-serialize | ~30% of 1 core per 1 kQPS of 10 KB responses | Streaming bodies where possible |\n3440\t| Global result merge | ~5% (min-heap; O(n log k)) | Dominated by hit count |\n3441\t| Rendezvous hash per doc | <1% | Tiny xxhash cost |\n3442\t| Filter DSL parse (§13.4) | <2% | Plan cached per (normalized filter, index) |\n3443\t| Anti-entropy fingerprint (§13.8) | self-throttled to <2% | Configurable |\n3444\t| Two-phase settings verify (§13.5) | negligible | Runs on settings change only |\n3445\t\n3446\tPer-pod peak throughput: **~3 kQPS for small searches (1 KB responses)** or **~1 kQPS for large searches (10 KB responses)** at 70% CPU utilization. Beyond that, add pods.\n3447\t\n3448\t### 14.4 Horizontal scaling: request path\n3449\t\n3450\tThe request path is strictly stateless. Any pod handles any request. Routing is deterministic from topology + config — every pod independently computes the same rendezvous assignment.\n3451\t\n3452\tKubernetes `Service` (ClusterIP) load-balances across pods; client affinity is not required. HPA scales the Deployment:\n3453\t\n3454\tThe HPA is only installed when `miroir.hpa.enabled: true` (default `false` in the dev chart values). Enabling the HPA requires `miroir.replicas >= 2` and `taskStore.backend: redis` — the chart's `values.schema.json` rejects configurations that violate either precondition, since SQLite is single-writer and an HPA that could scale to zero or one pod would defeat the production HA story.\n3455\t\n3456\t```yaml\n3457\tapiVersion: autoscaling/v2\n3458\tkind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler\n3459\tmetadata:\n3460\t  name: miroir\n3461\t  namespace: search\n3462\tspec:\n3463\t  scaleTargetRef:\n3464\t    apiVersion: apps/v1\n3465\t    kind: Deployment"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5f037661-e2ef-402d-b8b7-fdafb9852040","uuid":"3600a64a-5d4f-4129-8d98-26400b3ac925","timestamp":"2026-05-02T21:40:06.993Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/docs/plan/plan.md","content":"\n**Capabilities.**\n- **Instant-search** with 150 ms debounce; uses §13.10 query coalescing so concurrent keystrokes across users collapse to a single scatter\n- **Combined round-trip**: results + all facet counts fetched in a single `/multi-search` (§13.11) call per keystroke\n- **URL state**: every query + filter + sort + page is encoded in the URL so results are bookmarkable and shareable (`?q=laptop&brand=apple&price=500-2000&sort=price:asc&page=2`)\n- **Keyboard navigation**: `/` to focus the search box, arrow keys to move through results, `Enter` to open, `Esc` to clear\n- **Highlighting** of matched terms in title and snippet (uses Meilisearch's native `_formatted` output)\n- **Typo tolerance UI** with \"did you mean\" suggestions surfaced naturally when zero hits are returned\n- **Empty state** with popular queries (seeded from §13.18 canaries or operator-defined suggestions)\n- **Error states** with friendly, non-technical messaging and a \"report a problem\" link\n- **Pagination** — infinite scroll on mobile, classic pagination on desktop (configurable)\n- **Dark mode** via `prefers-color-scheme` and a manual toggle stored in `localStorage`\n- **Internationalization** — bundled with English; operator-supplied locale JSONs load on demand via `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/{lang}.json`\n\n**Design philosophy (beautiful and functional, desktop + mobile).**\n\n- **Aesthetic.** Content-first: the UI recedes, the results come forward. Generous whitespace. Strong typographic hierarchy — one variable-weight sans-serif (system-ui → Inter → sans-serif). Subtle motion design: 180 ms fade + translate on result-card enter; no gratuitous animation. Rounded card corners (12 px), soft shadows (1–3 px blur), thin dividers. back-gesture aware (browser back clears filters, not navigates away).\n  - **Tablet (640–1024 px)** — two-column: collapsible facets rail on the left, results on the right; card grid of 2 columns; toggle between card and list view.\n  - **Desktop (≥ 1024 px)** — three-column option: facets / results / optional preview pane; card grid of 3 or 4 columns; hover-preview on cards; keyboard shortcuts visible in footer; dense mode available.\n  - **Large desktop (≥ 1440 px)** — max content width clamp at 1440 px centered, preventing awkward stretched layouts on ultra-wide monitors.\n- **Accessibility.** WCAG 2.2 AA end-to-end: semantic HTML landmarks (`<search>`, `<main>`, `<nav>`); ARIA live region announces result counts after each query; all facets are labeled and reachable via Tab; focus rings always visible; color-blind-safe accent defaults; `prefers-reduced-motion` disables all motion; screen-reader tested flows for search, filter, and open-result.\n- **Performance.**\n  - Total initial payload ≤ 60 KB gzipped (HTML + JS + CSS + fonts)\n  - **Preact** (not React) + vanilla CSS custom properties\n  - No client-side bundler runtime; a single pre-built module\n  - System fonts default; custom web fonts loaded with `font-display: swap` and only if explicitly configured\n  - Images lazy-loaded with `loading=\"lazy\"` and `decoding=\"async\"`\n  - Layout-shift-free skeleton loaders during instant-search keystrokes\n  - Mobile network target: Lighthouse performance ≥ 95 on a mid-tier Android over 4G\n  - SSR-free — fully static SPA, cache-able at the edge\n- **Input modalities.** Touch on mobile (tap to select facet, tap card to open, pull-to-refresh), mouse on desktop (hover, right-click context menus optional), keyboard full coverage (`/`, arrows, Enter, Esc, `g f` to toggle filters).\n\n**Embeddable modes.**\n- **Iframe** — `<iframe src=\"https://search.example.com/ui/search/products?embed=true\">` strips chrome and adapts to container width; `postMessage` events for height auto-resize and result-clicked.\n- **Web component** — `<script src=\"https://search.example.com/ui/widget.js\"></script>` + `<miroir-search index=\"products\" accent=\"#2563eb\"></miroir-search>` drops a fully-featured search into any page.\n- **Headless mode** — `?headless=true` returns only the results container, no search input or facets, for use as part of a larger custom UI.\n\n**Custom templates.** `result_template: custom` allows operators to POST an HTML template with `{{field}}` / `{{#if}}` Handlebars-style interpolation. Templates are stored in the task store and served to the SPA at runtime. Template errors are caught and logged; the UI falls back to the default card template rather than breaking.\n\n**Security.**\n- Content-Security-Policy restricts script and style origins\n- All document fields are HTML-escaped before rendering (XSS-safe)\n- Rate limiting per source IP (configurable; default 60 rpm)\n- The SPA holds only a short-lived JWT session token; the search-scoped Meilisearch key is orchestrator-held and rotated (see \"Authentication model\" above) — never the master key, never the node key, never embedded in HTML\n- CORS configurable per deployment (see `search_ui.cors_allowed_origins` and `admin_ui.cors_allowed_origins` below)\n\n**Analytics (idempotent click-throughs).** When `search_ui.analytics.enabled: true`, the SPA emits a beacon on result click and on search-completion for end-user latency tracking via `POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/beacon` (see §4 Admin API table). Every click-through beacon carries an `event_id: <client-generated uuid>` in the body (§13.10 idempotency cache uses this field directly; the `Idempotency-Key` header is not required because the endpoint uses `event_id` as its dedup key); the browser synthesizes the UUID once per unique `(query, result_id, session)` tuple for click-throughs and `(session, minute_bucket)` for latency beacons, and reuses it on any retry — page refreshes that replay the same click from history do not double-count. The emitted CDC event (`type: click_through` or `type: latency`) uses `event_id` as its identity, and downstream consumers dedupe on that field. Latency events are subject to `cdc.emit_internal_writes` — operators who do not want end-user latency noise in their CDC stream can leave that flag at its default to suppress them. If the SPA cannot generate UUIDs (very old browsers without `crypto.randomUUID`), the orchestrator falls back to `hash(session_id || query || result_id || minute_bucket)` server-side. Event shape published to CDC:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"type\": \"click_through\",\n  \"event_id\": \"<idempotency key>\",\n  \"session_id\": \"<opaque session id>\",\n  \"query\": \"<query string>\",\n  \"result_id\": \"<primary key of clicked hit>\",\n  \"result_position\": 3,\n  \"index\": \"products\",\n  \"timestamp\": 1712345678901\n}\n```\n\n**Compatibility.** Purely orchestrator-served. All data flows through the existing search API. Uses §13.11 multi-search for results + facets; §13.6 session pinning for read-your-writes if the host app is doing concurrent writes; §13.10 query coalescing for efficient instant-search.\n\n**Config.**\n```yaml\nsearch_ui:\n  enabled: true\n  path: /ui/search\n  widget_script_enabled: true\n  embeddable: true\n\n  auth:\n    mode: public                    # public | shared_key | oauth_proxy\n    shared_key_env: \"\"              # env var holding the shared key (shared_key mode)\n    session_ttl_s: 900\n    session_rate_limit: \"10/minute\"\n    jwt_secret_env: SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET\n    oauth_proxy:\n      user_header: X-Forwarded-User\n      groups_header: X-Forwarded-Groups\n      filter_template: \"tenant IN [{groups}]\"   # null = disable filter injection\n      attribute_map:                            # optional per-claim binding\n        groups: groups_array\n        user: user_id_string\n\n  allowed_origins: [\"*\"]\n  scoped_key_max_age_days: 60                  # hard expiry on the scoped Meilisearch key (upper bound on key lifetime)\n  scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days: 30     # rotate this many days before the key's expiry (must be strictly less than scoped_key_max_age_days; see \"Config validation\" below)\n  scoped_key_rotation_drain_s: 120     # wait this long after the new generation is in place before revoking the previous key; see \"Revocation safety gate\" above\n\n  rate_limit:\n    per_ip: \"60/minute\"\n    backend: redis                  # redis | local (per-pod); redis required when replicas > 1\n    redis_key_prefix: \"miroir:ratelimit:searchui:\"\n    redis_ttl_s: 60\n\n  cors_allowed_origins: []          # empty = same-origin only\n  csp_overrides:\n    script_src: []                  # additional script-src domains\n    img_src: []                     # additional img-src domains\n    connect_src: []                 # additional connect-src for XHR targets\n  csp: \"default-src 'self'; img-src 'self' https:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'\"   # base template; any csp_overrides.* lists are merged into the corresponding directives at render time\n\n  analytics:\n    enabled: false\n    sink: cdc                     # publishes click-throughs as CDC events\n```\n\nWith `rate_limit.backend: local` and multi-pod deployments, the effective per-IP rate is `per_ip × pod_count` per minute cluster-wide (each pod maintains its own bucket table). For production deployments with `miroir.replicas > 1`, the Helm chart's `values.schema.json` rejects `backend: local` at install time, forcing Redis-backed shared state. The Redis bucket keys live under `redis_key_prefix` with `redis_ttl_s` TTL so memory stays bounded even under scan/spray attacks.\n\n**Filter injection in `oauth_proxy` mode.** When `search_ui.auth.oauth_proxy.filter_template` is non-null, Miroir automatically injects the rendered filter into every `/indexes/{uid}/search` call made under the session, using Meilisearch filter-concatenation semantics (the rendered clause is ANDed with any user-supplied filter the SPA sends — `(<user filter>) AND (<injected filter>)`). The `{groups}` placeholder is replaced at session-mint time with the comma-joined values of the `groups_header` (properly JSON-array-escaped for the Meilisearch filter DSL, e.g. `[\"engineering\",\"ops\"]`); the rendered claim is baked into the JWT so every subsequent search under that session enforces the same filter without re-reading proxy headers. `{user}` is replaced with the `user_header` value. `attribute_map` lets operators rename the JWT claim keys exposed to observability tooling and to the client (read-only — the SPA cannot override them). When `filter_template` is null, Miroir performs no filter injection — operators relying on a reverse proxy or downstream filter-at-app layer for access control must enforce it themselves; Miroir still forwards the user/group claims as read-only JWT claims for observability but does not gate results on them.\n\n**Metrics.** `miroir_search_ui_sessions_total`, `miroir_search_ui_queries_total{index}`, `miroir_search_ui_zero_hits_total{index}`, `miroir_search_ui_click_through_total{index}` (when analytics enabled), `miroir_search_ui_p95_ms{index}` (end-user-observed latency, measured via client beacon).\n\n---\n\n## 14. Resource Envelope and Horizontal Scaling\n\nMiroir is designed to fit within a fixed per-pod resource envelope. If aggregate workload exceeds what one pod can serve, Miroir scales **out**, not up — the orchestrator is stateless on the request path and partitions background work across pods by explicit design.\n\n### 14.1 Target envelope\n\nEach orchestrator pod is sized for:\n\n- **2 vCPU**\n- **3.75 GB RAM**\n\nThis matches common small-instance tiers (AWS t3.medium, GCP e2-medium, Hetzner CX22, Rackspace Spot 2c/3.75GB). Meilisearch nodes are sized independently per Section 6 and are unaffected by this envelope.\n\n### 14.2 Per-pod memory budget\n\nSteady-state allocation at default config, assuming all advanced capabilities enabled:\n\n| Component | Budget | Primary knob |\n|-----------|--------|--------------|\n| Rust/Tokio runtime + axum baseline | 80 MB | — |\n| HTTP/2 connection pools to nodes | 50 MB | `connection_pool_per_node` |\n| Request/response buffers (p99 concurrent) | 200 MB | `server.max_body_bytes`, `max_concurrent_requests` |\n| Task registry cache | 100 MB | `task_registry.cache_size` |\n| Idempotency cache (§13.10) | 100 MB | `idempotency.max_cached_keys` |\n| Session pinning state (§13.6) | 50 MB | `session_pinning.max_sessions` |\n| Query coalescing in-flight (§13.10) | 50 MB | `query_coalescing.max_subscribers` |\n| Router + EWMA state (§13.3) | 20 MB | fixed per node |\n| Query plan cache (§13.4) | 20 MB | fixed |\n| Alias table (§13.7) | 10 MB | fixed |\n| Metrics registry (prometheus) | 50 MB | fixed |\n| Dump import buffer (§13.9, only while importing) | 128 MB | `dump_import.memory_buffer_bytes` |\n| Anti-entropy fingerprinter (§13.8, only during pass) | 128 MB | `anti_entropy.max_read_concurrency` × batch |\n| Multi-search batch state (§13.11) | ~5 MB | `multi_search.max_queries_per_batch` (per-request cache) |\n| Vector search over-fetch scratch (§13.12) | ~30 MB | `vector_search.over_fetch_factor` (larger result buffers during merge) |\n| CDC in-memory batch buffer (§13.13, only when CDC enabled) | 64 MB | `cdc.buffer.memory_bytes` |\n| TTL sweeper state (§13.14) | ~5 MB | cursor per owned shard (negligible) |\n| Tenant map LRU (§13.15, only when `mode: api_key`) | ~20 MB | `tenant_affinity.mode` |\n| Shadow tee in-flight duplicates (§13.16) | varies (~50 MB worst case at 5% sample) | `shadow.targets[].sample_rate` × avg_response_size × concurrency |\n| Rollover policy evaluator (§13.17) | negligible | fixed |\n| Canary runner state (§13.18) | ~20 MB | `canary_runner.run_history_per_canary` (golden responses + run history LRU) |\n| Admin UI embedded assets (§13.19) | ~10 MB | fixed (rust-embed compressed + decompressed working set) |\n| Explain plan cache (§13.20) | ~10 MB | fixed |\n| Search UI embedded assets (§13.21) | ~10 MB | fixed |\n| Search UI rate-limiter state (§13.21) | ~20 MB (Redis-backed when `replicas > 1`) | `search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip` / `search_ui.rate_limit.backend` (IP buckets; see §13.21 rate-limiter shared-state note and §14.7 Redis memory accounting) |\n| Allocator overhead + headroom for bursts | 800 MB | — |\n| **Steady-state total (idle background)** | **~1.2 GB** | |\n| **Total with one heavy background job active** | **~1.7 GB** | |\n\nWith all new §13.11–21 features enabled simultaneously, steady-state budget rises by roughly **200 MB** versus the pre-§13.11 baseline, staying within the 3.75 GB envelope but tightening the burst headroom. Operators can disable individual capabilities (see §13 preamble — every `enabled: true` knob can be flipped off) to reclaim memory on constrained deployments. Remaining ~2 GB of the 3.75 GB envelope absorbs request concurrency spikes and GC/allocator behavior. Heavy background jobs (dump import, reshard backfill) only run on pods that have claimed them from the shared queue — not every pod simultaneously — so total budget stays within envelope even cluster-wide.\n\n### 14.3 Per-pod CPU budget\n\n| Path | Cost | Scaling |\n|------|------|---------|\n| Scatter fan-out dispatch | ~5% of 1 core per 1 kQPS | Linear in RG × RF |\n| JSON parse + re-serialize | ~30% of 1 core per 1 kQPS of 10 KB responses | Streaming bodies where possible |\n| Global result merge | ~5% (min-heap; O(n log k)) | Dominated by hit count |\n| Rendezvous hash per doc | <1% | Tiny xxhash cost |\n| Filter DSL parse (§13.4) | <2% | Plan cached per (normalized filter, index) |\n| Anti-entropy fingerprint (§13.8) | self-throttled to <2% | Configurable |\n| Two-phase settings verify (§13.5) | negligible | Runs on settings change only |\n\nPer-pod peak throughput: **~3 kQPS for small searches (1 KB responses)** or **~1 kQPS for large searches (10 KB responses)** at 70% CPU utilization. Beyond that, add pods.\n\n### 14.4 Horizontal scaling: request path\n\nThe request path is strictly stateless. Any pod handles any request. Routing is deterministic from topology + config — every pod independently computes the same rendezvous assignment.\n\nKubernetes `Service` (ClusterIP) load-balances across pods; client affinity is not required. HPA scales the Deployment:\n\nThe HPA is only installed when `miroir.hpa.enabled: true` (default `false` in the dev chart values). Enabling the HPA requires `miroir.replicas >= 2` and `taskStore.backend: redis` — the chart's `values.schema.json` rejects configurations that violate either precondition, since SQLite is single-writer and an HPA that could scale to zero or one pod would defeat the production HA story.\n\n```yaml\napiVersion: autoscaling/v2\nkind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler\nmetadata:\n  name: miroir\n  namespace: search\nspec:\n  scaleTargetRef:\n    apiVersion: apps/v1\n    kind: Deployment","numLines":200,"startLine":3266,"totalLines":3753}}}

    .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"expected HH:MM, got {}\", hm))?;\n54\t        let h: u16 = h.parse().map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid hour: {}\", h))?;\n55\t        let m: u16 = m.parse().map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid minute: {}\", m))?;\n56\t        if h >= 24 || m >= 60 {\n57\t            return Err(format!(\"time out of range: {}\", hm));\n58\t        }\n59\t        Ok(h * 60 + m)\n60\t    }\n61\t\n62\t    /// Does `utc_minutes` (minutes since midnight UTC) fall inside this window?\n63\t    pub fn contains(&self, utc_minutes: u16) -> bool {\n64\t        if self.start_mins <= self.end_mins {\n65\t            utc_minutes >= self.start_mins && utc_minutes < self.end_mins\n66\t        } else {\n67\t            // Wraps midnight, e.g. 22:00-06:00\n68\t            utc_minutes >= self.start_mins || utc_minutes < self.end_mins\n69\t        }\n70\t    }\n71\t}\n72\t\n73\t/// Resharding configuration (plan §13.1 + schedule window guard).\n74\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n75\tpub struct ReshardingConfig {\n76\t    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n77\t    pub enabled: bool,\n78\t    #[serde(default = \"default_backfill_concurrency\")]\n79\t    pub throttle_docs_per_sec: 0,\n114\t            verify_before_swap: true,\n115\t            retain_old_index_hours: default_retain_hours(),\n116\t            allowed_windows: Vec::new(),\n117\t        }\n118\t    }\n119\t}\n120\t\n121\t/// Result of the schedule window guard check.\n122\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n123\tpub enum WindowGuardResult {\n124\t    /// Current time is inside an allowed window.\n125\t    Allowed { window: String },\n126\t    /// No windows configured — always allowed.\n127\t    NoRestriction,\n128\t    /// Current time is outside all allowed windows.\n129\t    Denied {\n130\t        utc_now: String,\n131\t        allowed: Vec<String>,\n132\t    },\n133\t}\n134\t\n135\t/// Check whether resharding is allowed at the given UTC minute-of-day.\n136\t///\n137\t/// Returns `Allowed` if `utc_minute` falls inside any configured window,\n138\t/// `NoRestriction` if no windows are configured, or `Denied` otherwise.\n139\tpub fn check_window(utc_minute: u16, config: &ReshardingConfig) -> WindowGuardResult {\n140\t    if config.allowed_windows.is_empty() {\n141\t        return WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction;\n142\t    }\n143\t\n144\t    for raw in &config.allowed_windows {\n145\t        let window = match TimeWindow::parse(raw) {\n146\t            Ok(w) => w,\n147\t            Err(_) => continue,\n148\t        };\n149\t        if window.contains(utc_minute) {\n150\t            return WindowGuardResult::Allowed {\n151\t                window: raw.clone(),\n152\t            };\n153\t        }\n154\t    }\n155\t\n156\t    WindowGuardResult::Denied {\n157\t        utc_now: format!(\"{:02}:{:02} UTC\", utc_minute / 60, utc_minute % 60),\n158\t        allowed: config.allowed_windows.clone(),\n159\t    }\n160\t}\n161\t\n162\t/// Check the schedule window against the system clock.\n163\tpub fn check_window_now(config: &ReshardingConfig) -> WindowGuardResult {\n164\t    let utc_minute = current_utc_minute();\n165\t    check_window(utc_minute, config)\n166\t}\n167\t\n168\tfn current_utc_minute() -> u16 {\n169\t    let duration = SystemTime::now()\n170\t        .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n171\t        .unwrap_or_default();\n172\t    ((duration.as_secs() / 60) % (24 * 60)) as u16\n173\t}\n174\t\n175\t// pub total_bytes_written: u64,\n238\t\n239\t    /// Per-node peak storage (bytes).\n240\t    pub per_node_peak_storage_bytes: u64,\n241\t    /// Per-node normal storage (bytes).\n242\t    pub per_node_normal_storage_bytes: u64,\n243\t\n244\t    /// Hash distribution stats for old shards.\n245\t    pub old_shard_cv: f64,\n246\t    /// Hash distribution stats for new shards.\n247\t    pub new_shard_cv: f64,\n248\t}\n249\t\n250\t/// Run a resharding load simulation with the given parameters.\n251\t///\n252\t/// This models the six-phase resharding process from plan §13.1 using\n253\t/// the actual routing code to compute shard assignments and estimate\n254\t/// storage/write load. Document keys are synthesized for the corpus size\n255\t/// and routed through the real hash function to measure distribution.\n256\tpub fn simulate(params: &SimParams) -> SimResult {\n257\t    let total_docs = params.corpus_size_bytes / params.doc_size_bytes;\n258\t    let rf = params.replication_factor;\n259\t    let rg = params.replica_groups;\n260\t    let nodes_per_group = params.nodes_per_group;\n261\t\n262\t    // Build a synthetic topology for the simulation.\n263\t    let groups: Vec<Group> = (0..rg)\n264\t        .map(|g| {\n265\t            let mut group = Group::new(g);\n266\t            for n in 0..nodes_per_group {\n267\t                group.add_node(NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g{}-n{}\", g, n)));\n268\t            }\n269\t            group\n270\t        })\n271\t        .collect();\n272\t\n273\t    // Simulate document distribution across old and new shard counts.\n274\t    // Use the actual router hash to get realistic distribution.\n275\t    let mut old_shard_counts: Vec<u64> = vec![0; params.old_shards as usize];\n276\t    let mut new_shard_counts: Vec<u64> = vec![0; params.new_shards as usize];\n277\t\n278\t    // Track per-node storage for old and new shard assignments.\n279\t    // Each group stores the full corpus; each node in a group stores its\n280\t    // rendezvous-assigned fraction.\n281\t    let total_nodes = (rg as usize) * nodes_per_group;\n282\t    let mut node_storage_old: Vec<u64> = vec![0; total_nodes];\n283\t    let mut node_storage_new: Vec<u64> = vec![0; total_nodes];\n284\t\n285\t    for i in 0..total_docs {\n286\t        let key = format!(\"doc-{}\", i);\n287\t        let old_shard = shard_for_key(&key, params.old_shards);\n288\t        let new_shard = shard_for_key(&key, params.new_shards);\n289\t\n290\t        old_shard_counts[old_shard as usize] += 1;\n291\t        new_shard_counts[new_shard as usize] += 1;\n292\t\n293\t        // For each replica group, assign shard to RF nodes.\n294\t        for (g_idx, group) in groups.iter().enumerate() {\n295\t            let old_targets = assign_shard_in_group(old_shard, group.nodes(), rf);\n296\t            let new_targets = assign_shard_in_group(new_shard, group.nodes(), rf);\n297\t\n298\t            for node_id in &old_targets {\n299\t                let node_idx = g_idx * nodes_per_group\n300\t                    + group.nodes().iter().position(|n| n == node_id).unwrap_or(0);\n301\t                node_storage_old[node_idx] += params.doc_size_bytes;\n302\t            }\n303\t            for node_id in &new_targets {\n304\t                let node_idx = g_idx * nodes_per_group\n305\t                    + group.nodes().iter().position(|n| n == node_id).unwrap_or(0);\n306\t                node_storage_new[node_idx] += params.doc_size_bytes;\n307\t            }\n308\t        }\n309\t    }\n310\t\n311\t    // Compute distribution coefficients of variation.\n312\t    let old_cv = cv(&old_shard_counts);\n313\t    let new_cv = cv(&new_shard_counts);\n314\t\n315\t    // Normal storage: corpus replicated across RG groups.\n316\t    let normal_storage_bytes = params.corpus_size_bytes * rg as u64;\n317\t    // Peak storage: live + shadow (both fully populated).\n318\t    let peak_storage_bytes = normal_storage_bytes * 2;\n319\t\n320\t    // Per-node storage (max across all nodes).\n321\t    let per_node_normal = node_storage_old.iter().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);\n322\t    let per_node_peak = per_node_normal + node_storage_new.iter().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);\n323\t\n324\t    // Write rates.\n325\t    // Normal: each incoming doc → RF × RG actual node writes.\n326\t    let normal_write_rate = params.write_rate_dps * rf as u64 * rg as u64;\n327\t    // Dual-write: each incoming doc → 2 × (RF × RG) writes (old + new assignment).\n328\t    let dual_write_rate = normal_write_rate * 2;\n329\t    // Backfill: reads all docs, writes each to new assignment → RF × RG writes/doc.\n330\t    // Plus ongoing dual-writes for new incoming docs.\n331\t    let backfill_write_rate = params.backfill_throttle_dps * rf as u64 * rg as u64;\n332\t    let peak_write_rate = dual_write_rate + backfill_write_rate;\n333\t\n334\t    let dual_write_amplification = 2.0;\n335\t    let peak_write_amplification = peak_write_rate as f64 / normal_write_rate as f64;\n336\t\n337\t    // Backfill duration: total docs / throttle rate.\n338\t    let backfill_duration_secs = if params.backfill_throttle_dps > 0 {\n339\t        total_docs as f64 / params.backfill_throttle_dps as f64\n340\t    } else {\n341\t        f64::INFINITY\n342\t    };\n343\t\n344\t    // Total bytes written during reshard:\n345\t    // 1. Dual-write ongoing for the full reshard duration.\n346\t    // 2. Backfill writes of entire corpus.\n347\t    // Approximate: backfill_duration × dual_write_rate + corpus × RF × RG.\n348\t    let total_reshard_write_bytes = if params.backfill_throttle_dps > 0 {\n349\t        let dual_write_bytes =\n350\t            backfill_duration_secs * dual_write_rate as f64 * params.doc_size_bytes as f64;\n351\t        let backfill_bytes = total_docs * rf as u64 * rg as u64 * params.doc_size_bytes;\n352\t        (dual_write_bytes as u64) + backfill_bytes\n353\t    } else {\n354\t        0\n355\t    };\n356\t\n357\t    let storage_amplification = peak_storage_bytes as f64 / normal_storage_bytes as f64;\n358\t\n359\t    SimResult {\n360\t        label: format!(\n361\t            \"{}KB/{}GB/RG{}/RF{}\",\n362\t            params.doc_size_bytes / 1024,\n363\t            params.corpus_size_bytes / (1024 * 1024 * 1024),\n364\t            rg,\n365\t            rf\n366\t        ),\n367\t        doc_size_bytes: params.doc_size_bytes,\n368\t        corpus_size_bytes: params.corpus_size_bytes,\n369\t        total_docs,\n370\t        replica_groups: rg,\n371\t        replication_factor: rf,\n372\t        old_shards: params.old_shards,\n373\t        new_shards: params.new_shards,\n374\t        nodes_per_group,\n375\t        write_rate_dps: params.write_rate_dps,\n376\t\n377\t        normal_storage_bytes,\n378\t        peak_storage_bytes,\n379\t        storage_amplification,\n380\t\n381\t        normal_write_rate,\n382\t        dual_write_rate,\n383\t        peak_write_rate,\n384\t        dual_write_amplification,\n385\t        peak_write_amplification,\n386\t\n387\t        backfill_duration_secs,\n388\t        total_bytes_written: total_reshard_write_bytes,\n389\t\n390\t        per_node_peak_storage_bytes: per_node_peak,\n391\t        per_node_normal_storage_bytes: per_node_normal,\n392\t\n393\t        old_shard_cv: old_cv,\n394\t        new_shard_cv: new_cv,\n395\t    }\n396\t}\n397\t\n398\t/// Coefficient of variation for a distribution.\n399\tfn cv(values: &[u64]) -> f64 {\n400\t    if values.is_empty() {\n401\t        return 0.0;\n402\t    }\n403\t    let n = values.len() as f64;\n404\t    let mean = values.iter().sum::<u64>() as f64 / n;\n405\t    if mean == 0.0 {\n406\t        return 0.0;\n407\t    }\n408\t    let variance = values\n409\t        .iter()\n410\t        .map(|v| (*v as f64 - mean).powi(2))\n411\t        .sum::<f64>()\n412\t        / n;\n413\t    variance.sqrt() / mean\n414\t}\n415\t\n416\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n417\t// Tests\n418\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n419\t\n420\t#[cfg(test)]\n421\tmod tests {\n422\t    use super::*;\n423\t\n424\t    // ---- TimeWindow parsing and containment ----\n425\t\n426\t    #[test]\n427\t    fn time_window_parse_simple() {\n428\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n429\t        assert_eq!(w.start_mins, 120);\n430\t        assert_eq!(w.end_mins, 360);\n431\t    }\n432\t\n433\t    #[test]\n434\t    fn time_window_parse_wrap_midnight() {\n435\t        let w =    }\n455\t\n456\t    #[test]\n457\t    fn time_window_boundary_start() {\n458\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n459\t        assert!(w.contains(120)); // exactly }\n474\t\n475\t    // ---- Window guard ----\n476\t\n477\t    #[test]\n478\t    fn window_guard_no_restriction() {\n479\t        let config = ReshardingConfig::default();\n480\t assert!(matches!(result, WindowGuardResult::Allowed { .. }));\n491\t    }\n492\t\n493\t    #[test]\n494\t    fn window_guard_denied() {\n495\t        let config = ReshardingConfig {\n496\t assert!(matches!(result, WindowGuardResult::Denied { .. }));\n501\t    }\n502\t\n503\t    #[test]\n504\t    fn window_guard_multiple_windows() {\n505\t        let config = ReshardingConfig {\n506\t            allowed_windows: vec![\"02:00-04:00\".into(), \"22:00-23:30\".into()],\n507\t            ..Default::default()\n508\t        };\n509\t        // In first window.\n510\t   #[test]\n529\t    fn simulation_storage_always_2x() {\n530\t        // Regardless of parameters, peak storage should be exactly 2× normal.\n531\t        let params = SimParams {\n532\t };\n542\t        let result = simulate(&params);\n543\t        assert!(\n544\t            (result.storage_amplification - 2.0).abs() < 0.01,\n545\t            \"expected ~2.0, got {}\",\n546\t            result.storage_amplification\n547\t        );\n548\t    }\n549\t\n550\t    #[test]\n551\t    fn simulation_dual_write_is_2x() {\n552\t        let params = SimParams {\n553\t       old_shards: 4,\n637\t            new_shards: 8,\n638\t            nodes_per_group: 2,\n639\t            backfill_throttle_dps: 0,\n640\t        };\n641\t        let result = cv_empty_and_zero() {\n648\t        assert_eq!(cv(&[]), 0.0);\n649\t        assert_eq!(cv(&[0, 0, 0]), 0.0);\n650\t    }\n651\t}\n652\t\n653\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n654\t// Six-phase resharding execution (plan §13.1)\n655\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n656\t\n657\t/// Resharding phase identifiers (matching plan §13.1 steps).\n658\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n659\t#[repr(u8)]\n660\tpub enum ReshardPhase {\n661\t    /// No active resharding.\n662\t    Idle = 0,\n663\t    /// Phase 1: Shadow index created.\n664\t    ShadowCreated = 1,\n665\t    /// Phase 2: Dual-hash dual-write active.\n666\t    DualWriteActive = 2,\n667\t    /// Phase 3: Backfill in progress.\n668\t    BackfillInProgress = 3,\n669\t    /// Phase 4: Verification in progress.\n670\t    Verifying = 4,\n671\t    /// Phase 5: Alias swap completed.\n672\t    Swapped = 5,\n673\t    /// Phase 6: Cleanup in progress.\n674\t    CleaningUp = 6,\n675\t    /// Resharding completed successfully.\n676\t    Complete = 7,\n677\t    /// Resharding failed.\n678\t    Failed = 8,\n679\t}\n680\t\n681\timpl ReshardPhase {\n682\t    /// Human-readable phase name.\n683\t    pub fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n684\t        match self {\n685\t            Self::Idle => \"Idle\",\n686\t            Self::ShadowCreated => \"Shadow Created\",\n687\t            Self::DualWriteActive => \"Dual-Write Active\",\n688\t            Self::BackfillInProgress => \"Backfill In Progress\",\n689\t            Self::Verifying => \"Verifying\",\n690\t            Self::Swapped => \"Swapped\",\n691\t            Self::CleaningUp => \"Cleaning Up\",\n692\t            Self::Complete => \"Complete\",\n693\t            Self::Failed => \"Failed\",\n694\t        }\n695\t    }\n696\t\n697\t    /// Parse from u8 (for storage).\n698\t    pub fn from_u8(v: u8) -> Option<Self> {\n699\t        match v {\n700\t            0 => Some(Self::Idle),\n701\t            1 => Some(Self::ShadowCreated),\n702\t            2 => Some(Self::DualWriteActive),\n703\t            3 => Some(Self::BackfillInProgress),\n704\t            4 => Some(Self::Verifying),\n705\t            5 => Some(Self::Swapped),\n706\t            6 => Some(Self::CleaningUp),\n707\t            7 => Some(Self::Complete),\n708\t            8 => Some(Self::Failed),\n709\t            _ => None,\n710\t        }\n711\t    }\n712\t}\n713\t\n714\t/// Active resharding operation state.\n715\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n716\tpub struct ReshardOperation {\n717\t    /// Unique operation ID.\n718\t    pub id: String,\n719\t    /// Index UID being resharded.\n720\t    pub index_uid: String,\n721\t    /// Old shard count.\n722\t    pub old_shards: u32,\n723\t    /// New shard count.\n724\t    pub target_shards: u32,\n725\t    /// Current phase.\n726\t    pub phase: ReshardPhase,\n727\t    /// Phase started at (UNIX ms).\n728\t    pub phase_started_at: u64,\n729\t    /// Operation created at (UNIX ms).\n730\t    pub created_at: u64,\n731\t    /// Documents backfilled so far.\n732\t    pub documents_backfilled: u64,\n733\t    /// Total documents to backfill (estimated at start).\n734\t    pub total_documents: u64,\n735\t    /// Last error message (if any).\n736\t    pub last_error: Option<String>,\n737\t    /// Shadow index UID.\n738\t    pub shadow_index: String,\n739\t    /// Verification results (populated after phase 4).\n740\t    pub verification_results: Option<VerificationResults>,\n741\t    /// Cleanup retention deadline (UNIX ms).\n742\t    pub cleanup_deadline: Option<u64>,\n743\t}\n744\t\n745\t/// Results from phase 4 verification.\n746\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n747\tpub struct VerificationResults {\n748\t    /// Live index PK set size.\n749\t    pub live_pk_count: u64,\n750\t    /// Shadow index PK set size.\n751\t    pub shadow_pk_count: u64,\n752\t    /// PKs only in live index.\n753\t    pub live_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n754\t    /// PKs only in shadow index.\n755\t    pub shadow_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n756\t    /// PKs with content hash mismatch.\n757\t    pub mismatched_pks: Vec<String>,\n758\t    /// Whether verification passed.\n759\t    pub passed: bool,\n760\t}\n761\t\n762\timpl ReshardOperation {\n763\t    /// Create a new resharding operation.\n764\t    pub fn new(index_uid: String, old_shards: u32, target_shards: u32) -> Self {\n765\t        let id = format!(\"reshard-{}-{}\", index_uid, uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n766\t        let shadow_index = format!(\"{}__reshard_{}\", index_uid, target_shards);\n767\t        let now = millis_now();\n768\t        Self {\n769\t            id,\n770\t            index_uid,\n771\t            old_shards,\n772\t            target_shards,\n773\t            phase: ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated,\n774\t            phase_started_at: now,\n775\t            created_at: now,\n776\t            documents_backfilled: 0,\n777\t            total_documents: 0,\n778\t            last_error: None,\n779\t            shadow_index,\n780\t            verification_results: None,\n781\t            cleanup_deadline: None,\n782\t        }\n783\t    }\n784\t\n785\t    /// Transition to the next phase.\n786\t    pub fn advance_phase(&mut self, new_phase: ReshardPhase) {\n787\t        self.phase = new_phase;\n788\t        self.phase_started_at = millis_now();\n789\t    }\n790\t\n791\t    /// Record an error and mark as failed.\n792\t    pub fn fail(&mut self, error: String) {\n793\t        self.last_error = Some(error);\n794\t        self.phase = ReshardPhase::Failed;\n795\t    }\n796\t\n797\t    /// Update backfill progress.\n798\t    pub fn update_backfill_progress(&mut self, backfilled: u64, total: u64) {\n799\t        self.documents_backfilled = backfilled;\n800\t        self.total_documents = total;\n801\t    }\n802\t\n803\t    /// Calculate backfill progress ratio (0.0 to 1.0).\n804\t    pub fn backfill_progress(&self) -> f64 {\n805\t        if self.total_documents == 0 {\n806\t            return 0.0;\n807\t        }\n808\t        (self.documents_backfilled as f64) / (self.total_documents as f64)\n809\t    }\n810\t\n811\t    /// Check if the operation is in a terminal state.\n812\t    pub fn is_terminal(&self) -> bool {\n813\t        matches!(\n814\t            self.phase,\n815\t            ReshardPhase::Complete | ReshardPhase::Failed\n816\t        )\n817\t    }\n818\t\n819\t    /// Get the shadow index name.\n820\t    pub fn shadow_index_name(&self) -> &str {\n821\t        &self.shadow_index\n822\t    }\n823\t}\n824\t\n825\t/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\n826\tfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n827\t    SystemTime::now()\n828\t        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n829\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n830\t        .as_millis() as u64\n831\t}\n832\t\n833\t/// In-memory registry of active resharding operations.\n834\t///\n835\t/// In production, this is persisted to the task store (SQLite/Redis).\n836\t/// This in-memory version is for single-pod development.\n837\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n838\tpub struct ReshardRegistry {\n839\t    operations: HashMap<String, ReshardOperation>,\n840\t    /// Index UID -> active operation ID (at most one per index).\n841\t    index_ops: HashMap<String, String>,\n842\t}\n843\t\n844\timpl ReshardRegistry {\n845\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n846\t        Self::default()\n847\t    }\n848\t\n849\t    /// Register a new resharding operation.\n850\t    pub fn register(&mut self, op: ReshardOperation) -> Result<(), String> {\n851\t        if let Some(existing_id) = self.index_ops.get(&op.index_uid) {\n852\t            return Err(format!(\n853\t                \"Resharding already in progress for index '{}': {}\",\n854\t                op.index_uid, existing_id\n855\t            ));\n856\t        }\n857\t        self.index_ops.insert(op.index_uid.clone(), op.id.clone());\n858\t        self.operations.insert(op.id.clone(), op);\n859\t        Ok(())\n860\t    }\n861\t\n862\t    /// Get an operation by ID.\n863\t    pub fn get(&self, id: &str) -> Option<&ReshardOperation> {\n864\t        self.operations.get(id)\n865\t    }\n866\t\n867\t    /// Get mutable reference for updates.\n868\t    pub fn get_mut(&mut self, id: &str) -> Option<&mut ReshardOperation> {\n869\t        self.operations.get_mut(id)\n870\t    }\n871\t\n872\t    /// Get the active operation for an index (if any).\n873\t    pub fn get_for_index(&self, index_uid: &str) -> Option<&ReshardOperation> {\n874\t        self.index_ops\n875\t            .get(index_uid)\n876\t            .and_then(|id| self.operations.get(id))\n877\t    }\n878\t\n879\t    /// Update an operation.\n880\t    pub fn update(&mut self, op: ReshardOperation) -> Result<(), String> {\n881\t        if !self.operations.contains_key(&op.id) {\n882\t            return Err(format!(\"Operation '{}' not found\", op.id));\n883\t        }\n884\t        self.operations.insert(op.id.clone(), op);\n885\t        Ok(())\n886\t    }\n887\t\n888\t    /// Complete an operation and remove from active index tracking.\n889\t    pub fn complete(&mut self, id: &str) -> Result<(), String> {\n890\t        let op = self.operations.get(id).ok_or_else(|| format!(\"Operation '{}' not found\", id))?;\n891\t        if !op.is_terminal() {\n892\t            return Err(format!(\"Operation '{}' is not in a terminal state\", id));\n893\t        }\n894\t        self.index_ops.remove(&op.index_uid);\n895\t        Ok(())\n896\t    }\n897\t\n898\t    /// List all operations.\n899\t    pub fn list(&self) -> Vec<&ReshardOperation> {\n900\t        self.operations.values().collect()\n901\t    }\n902\t\n903\t    /// Clean up completed operations older than the retention period.\n904\t    pub fn prune_completed(&mut self, max_age_ms: u64) {\n905\t        let now = millis_now();\n906\t        let mut to_remove = Vec::new();\n907\t        for (id, op) in &self.operations {\n908\t            if op.is_terminal() && (now.saturating_sub(op.created_at) > max_age_ms) {\n909\t                to_remove.push(id.clone());\n910\t                self.index_ops.remove(&op.index_uid);\n911\t            }\n912\t        }\n913\t        for id in to_remove {\n914\t            self.operations.remove(&id);\n915\t        }\n916\t    }\n917\t}\n918\t\n919\t#[cfg(test)]\n920\tmod tests_reshard_execution {\n921\t    use super::*;\n922\t\n923\t    #[test]\n924\t    fn phase_display_names() {\n925\t        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::Idle.name(), \"Idle\");\n926\t        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::BackfillInProgress.name(),       ReshardPhase::Verifying,\n938\t            ReshardPhase::Swapped,\n939\t            ReshardPhase::CleaningUp,\n940\t            ReshardPhase::Complete,\n941\t   let retrieved = reg.get_for_index(\"products\").unwrap();\n994\t        assert_eq!(retrieved.id, id);\n995\t    }\n996\t\n997\t    #[test]\n998\t    fn registry_complete_releases_index() {\n999\t        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n1000\t        let op = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 64, 128);\n1001\t        let id = op.id.clone();\n1002\t
