}\n1021\t}\n1022\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5f037661-e2ef-402d-b8b7-fdafb9852040","uuid":"6ac32e8d-eb7d-4d01-886f-598900425d3b","timestamp":"2026-05-02T21:40:08.422Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/reshard.rs","content":"//! Online resharding: window guard, simulation model, and six-phase execution.\n//!\n//! Implements the plan §13.1 shadow-index resharding mechanics and §15 OP#3\n//! empirical validation of the 2× transient load caveat.\n\nuse crate::router::{assign_shard_in_group, shard_for_key};\nuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Schedule window guard\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// A UTC time window like `\"02:00-06:00\"`.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct TimeWindow {\n    /// Start hour+minute in minutes since midnight UTC.\n    pub start_mins: u16,\n    /// End hour+minute in minutes since midnight UTC.\n    pub end_mins: u16,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for TimeWindow {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"{:02}:{:02}-{:02}:{:02}\",\n            self.start_mins / 60,\n            self.start_mins % 60,\n            self.end_mins / 60,\n            self.end_mins % 60\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl TimeWindow {\n    /// Parse a `\"HH:MM-HH:MM\"` string (UTC).\n    pub fn parse(s: &str) -> Result<Self, String> {\n        let (start, end) = s\n            .split_once('-')\n            .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"expected HH:MM-HH:MM, got {}\", s))?;\n        Ok(TimeWindow {\n            start_mins: Self::parse_hm(start)?,\n            end_mins: Self::parse_hm(end)?,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn parse_hm(hm: &str) -> Result<u16, String> {\n        let (h, m) = hm\n            .split_once(':')\n            .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"expected HH:MM, got {}\", hm))?;\n        let h: u16 = h.parse().map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid hour: {}\", h))?;\n        let m: u16 = m.parse().map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid minute: {}\", m))?;\n        if h >= 24 || m >= 60 {\n            return Err(format!(\"time out of range: {}\", hm));\n        }\n        Ok(h * 60 + m)\n    }\n\n    /// Does `utc_minutes` (minutes since midnight UTC) fall inside this window?\n    pub fn contains(&self, utc_minutes: u16) -> bool {\n        if self.start_mins <= self.end_mins {\n            utc_minutes >= self.start_mins && utc_minutes < self.end_mins\n        } else {\n            // Wraps midnight, e.g. 22:00-06:00\n            utc_minutes >= self.start_mins || utc_minutes < self.end_mins\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Resharding configuration (plan §13.1 + schedule window guard).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ReshardingConfig {\n    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    #[serde(default = \"default_backfill_concurrency\")]\n    pub backfill_concurrency: usize,\n    #[serde(default = \"default_backfill_batch_size\")]\n    pub backfill_batch_size: usize,\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub throttle_docs_per_sec: u64,\n    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n    pub verify_before_swap: bool,\n    #[serde(default = \"default_retain_hours\")]\n    pub retain_old_index_hours: u64,\n    /// Allowed schedule windows in `\"HH:MM-HH:MM UTC\"` format.\n    /// Empty means any time is allowed (no restriction).\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub allowed_windows: Vec<String>,\n}\n\nfn default_backfill_concurrency() -> usize {\n    4\n}\nfn default_backfill_batch_size() -> usize {\n    1000\n}\nfn default_true() -> bool {\n    true\n}\nfn default_retain_hours() -> u64 {\n    48\n}\n\nimpl Default for ReshardingConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            backfill_concurrency: default_backfill_concurrency(),\n            backfill_batch_size: default_backfill_batch_size(),\n            throttle_docs_per_sec: 0,\n            verify_before_swap: true,\n            retain_old_index_hours: default_retain_hours(),\n            allowed_windows: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result of the schedule window guard check.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum WindowGuardResult {\n    /// Current time is inside an allowed window.\n    Allowed { window: String },\n    /// No windows configured — always allowed.\n    NoRestriction,\n    /// Current time is outside all allowed windows.\n    Denied {\n        utc_now: String,\n        allowed: Vec<String>,\n    },\n}\n\n/// Check whether resharding is allowed at the given UTC minute-of-day.\n///\n/// Returns `Allowed` if `utc_minute` falls inside any configured window,\n/// `NoRestriction` if no windows are configured, or `Denied` otherwise.\npub fn check_window(utc_minute: u16, config: &ReshardingConfig) -> WindowGuardResult {\n    if config.allowed_windows.is_empty() {\n        return WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction;\n    }\n\n    for raw in &config.allowed_windows {\n        let window = match TimeWindow::parse(raw) {\n            Ok(w) => w,\n            Err(_) => continue,\n        };\n        if window.contains(utc_minute) {\n            return WindowGuardResult::Allowed {\n                window: raw.clone(),\n            };\n        }\n    }\n\n    WindowGuardResult::Denied {\n        utc_now: format!(\"{:02}:{:02} UTC\", utc_minute / 60, utc_minute % 60),\n        allowed: config.allowed_windows.clone(),\n    }\n}\n\n/// Check the schedule window against the system clock.\npub fn check_window_now(config: &ReshardingConfig) -> WindowGuardResult {\n    let utc_minute = current_utc_minute();\n    check_window(utc_minute, config)\n}\n\nfn current_utc_minute() -> u16 {\n    let duration = SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n    ((duration.as_secs() / 60) % (24 * 60)) as u16\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Resharding load simulation\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Parameters for a single simulation run.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct SimParams {\n    /// Document size in bytes.\n    pub doc_size_bytes: u64,\n    /// Total corpus size in bytes.\n    pub corpus_size_bytes: u64,\n    /// Incoming write rate in documents per second.\n    pub write_rate_dps: u64,\n    /// Number of replica groups.\n    pub replica_groups: u32,\n    /// Replication factor (intra-group copies per shard).\n    pub replication_factor: usize,\n    /// Old shard count (before reshard).\n    pub old_shards: u32,\n    /// New shard count (after reshard).\n    pub new_shards: u32,\n    /// Number of nodes per replica group.\n    pub nodes_per_group: usize,\n    /// Backfill throttle in documents per second (0 = unlimited).\n    pub backfill_throttle_dps: u64,\n}\n\n/// Results from a single simulation run.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SimResult {\n    pub label: String,\n    pub doc_size_bytes: u64,\n    pub corpus_size_bytes: u64,\n    pub total_docs: u64,\n    pub replica_groups: u32,\n    pub replication_factor: usize,\n    pub old_shards: u32,\n    pub new_shards: u32,\n    pub nodes_per_group: usize,\n    pub write_rate_dps: u64,\n\n    /// Normal steady-state storage across entire cluster (bytes).\n    pub normal_storage_bytes: u64,\n    /// Peak storage during resharding (live + shadow full, bytes).\n    pub peak_storage_bytes: u64,\n    /// Storage amplification factor (peak / normal).\n    pub storage_amplification: f64,\n\n    /// Normal steady-state write rate (actual node writes/sec).\n    pub normal_write_rate: u64,\n    /// Dual-write rate (phase 2 only, no backfill).\n    pub dual_write_rate: u64,\n    /// Peak write rate during backfill + dual-write.\n    pub peak_write_rate: u64,\n    /// Write amplification factor during dual-write only.\n    pub dual_write_amplification: f64,\n    /// Write amplification factor during peak (backfill + dual-write).\n    pub peak_write_amplification: f64,\n\n    /// Backfill duration in seconds (at configured throttle).\n    pub backfill_duration_secs: f64,\n    /// Total bytes written during full reshard operation.\n    pub total_bytes_written: u64,\n\n    /// Per-node peak storage (bytes).\n    pub per_node_peak_storage_bytes: u64,\n    /// Per-node normal storage (bytes).\n    pub per_node_normal_storage_bytes: u64,\n\n    /// Hash distribution stats for old shards.\n    pub old_shard_cv: f64,\n    /// Hash distribution stats for new shards.\n    pub new_shard_cv: f64,\n}\n\n/// Run a resharding load simulation with the given parameters.\n///\n/// This models the six-phase resharding process from plan §13.1 using\n/// the actual routing code to compute shard assignments and estimate\n/// storage/write load. Document keys are synthesized for the corpus size\n/// and routed through the real hash function to measure distribution.\npub fn simulate(params: &SimParams) -> SimResult {\n    let total_docs = params.corpus_size_bytes / params.doc_size_bytes;\n    let rf = params.replication_factor;\n    let rg = params.replica_groups;\n    let nodes_per_group = params.nodes_per_group;\n\n    // Build a synthetic topology for the simulation.\n    let groups: Vec<Group> = (0..rg)\n        .map(|g| {\n            let mut group = Group::new(g);\n            for n in 0..nodes_per_group {\n                group.add_node(NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g{}-n{}\", g, n)));\n            }\n            group\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Simulate document distribution across old and new shard counts.\n    // Use the actual router hash to get realistic distribution.\n    let mut old_shard_counts: Vec<u64> = vec![0; params.old_shards as usize];\n    let mut new_shard_counts: Vec<u64> = vec![0; params.new_shards as usize];\n\n    // Track per-node storage for old and new shard assignments.\n    // Each group stores the full corpus; each node in a group stores its\n    // rendezvous-assigned fraction.\n    let total_nodes = (rg as usize) * nodes_per_group;\n    let mut node_storage_old: Vec<u64> = vec![0; total_nodes];\n    let mut node_storage_new: Vec<u64> = vec![0; total_nodes];\n\n    for i in 0..total_docs {\n        let key = format!(\"doc-{}\", i);\n        let old_shard = shard_for_key(&key, params.old_shards);\n        let new_shard = shard_for_key(&key, params.new_shards);\n\n        old_shard_counts[old_shard as usize] += 1;\n        new_shard_counts[new_shard as usize] += 1;\n\n        // For each replica group, assign shard to RF nodes.\n        for (g_idx, group) in groups.iter().enumerate() {\n            let old_targets = assign_shard_in_group(old_shard, group.nodes(), rf);\n            let new_targets = assign_shard_in_group(new_shard, group.nodes(), rf);\n\n            for node_id in &old_targets {\n                let node_idx = g_idx * nodes_per_group\n                    + group.nodes().iter().position(|n| n == node_id).unwrap_or(0);\n                node_storage_old[node_idx] += params.doc_size_bytes;\n            }\n            for node_id in &new_targets {\n                let node_idx = g_idx * nodes_per_group\n                    + group.nodes().iter().position(|n| n == node_id).unwrap_or(0);\n                node_storage_new[node_idx] += params.doc_size_bytes;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Compute distribution coefficients of variation.\n    let old_cv = cv(&old_shard_counts);\n    let new_cv = cv(&new_shard_counts);\n\n    // Normal storage: corpus replicated across RG groups.\n    let normal_storage_bytes = params.corpus_size_bytes * rg as u64;\n    // Peak storage: live + shadow (both fully populated).\n    let peak_storage_bytes = normal_storage_bytes * 2;\n\n    // Per-node storage (max across all nodes).\n    let per_node_normal = node_storage_old.iter().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);\n    let per_node_peak = per_node_normal + node_storage_new.iter().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Write rates.\n    // Normal: each incoming doc → RF × RG actual node writes.\n    let normal_write_rate = params.write_rate_dps * rf as u64 * rg as u64;\n    // Dual-write: each incoming doc → 2 × (RF × RG) writes (old + new assignment).\n    let dual_write_rate = normal_write_rate * 2;\n    // Backfill: reads all docs, writes each to new assignment → RF × RG writes/doc.\n    // Plus ongoing dual-writes for new incoming docs.\n    let backfill_write_rate = params.backfill_throttle_dps * rf as u64 * rg as u64;\n    let peak_write_rate = dual_write_rate + backfill_write_rate;\n\n    let dual_write_amplification = 2.0;\n    let peak_write_amplification = peak_write_rate as f64 / normal_write_rate as f64;\n\n    // Backfill duration: total docs / throttle rate.\n    let backfill_duration_secs = if params.backfill_throttle_dps > 0 {\n        total_docs as f64 / params.backfill_throttle_dps as f64\n    } else {\n        f64::INFINITY\n    };\n\n    // Total bytes written during reshard:\n    // 1. Dual-write ongoing for the full reshard duration.\n    // 2. Backfill writes of entire corpus.\n    // Approximate: backfill_duration × dual_write_rate + corpus × RF × RG.\n    let total_reshard_write_bytes = if params.backfill_throttle_dps > 0 {\n        let dual_write_bytes =\n            backfill_duration_secs * dual_write_rate as f64 * params.doc_size_bytes as f64;\n        let backfill_bytes = total_docs * rf as u64 * rg as u64 * params.doc_size_bytes;\n        (dual_write_bytes as u64) + backfill_bytes\n    } else {\n        0\n    };\n\n    let storage_amplification = peak_storage_bytes as f64 / normal_storage_bytes as f64;\n\n    SimResult {\n        label: format!(\n            \"{}KB/{}GB/RG{}/RF{}\",\n            params.doc_size_bytes / 1024,\n            params.corpus_size_bytes / (1024 * 1024 * 1024),\n            rg,\n            rf\n        ),\n        doc_size_bytes: params.doc_size_bytes,\n        corpus_size_bytes: params.corpus_size_bytes,\n        total_docs,\n        replica_groups: rg,\n        replication_factor: rf,\n        old_shards: params.old_shards,\n        new_shards: params.new_shards,\n        nodes_per_group,\n        write_rate_dps: params.write_rate_dps,\n\n        normal_storage_bytes,\n        peak_storage_bytes,\n        storage_amplification,\n\n        normal_write_rate,\n        dual_write_rate,\n        peak_write_rate,\n        dual_write_amplification,\n        peak_write_amplification,\n\n        backfill_duration_secs,\n        total_bytes_written: total_reshard_write_bytes,\n\n        per_node_peak_storage_bytes: per_node_peak,\n        per_node_normal_storage_bytes: per_node_normal,\n\n        old_shard_cv: old_cv,\n        new_shard_cv: new_cv,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Coefficient of variation for a distribution.\nfn cv(values: &[u64]) -> f64 {\n    if values.is_empty() {\n        return 0.0;\n    }\n    let n = values.len() as f64;\n    let mean = values.iter().sum::<u64>() as f64 / n;\n    if mean == 0.0 {\n        return 0.0;\n    }\n    let variance = values\n        .iter()\n        .map(|v| (*v as f64 - mean).powi(2))\n        .sum::<f64>()\n        / n;\n    variance.sqrt() / mean\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    // ---- TimeWindow parsing and containment ----\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_parse_simple() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(w.start_mins, 120);\n        assert_eq!(w.end_mins, 360);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_parse_wrap_midnight() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"22:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(w.start_mins, 1320);\n        assert_eq!(w.end_mins, 360);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_contains_normal() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert!(w.contains(180)); // 03:00\n        assert!(!w.contains(100)); // 01:40\n        assert!(!w.contains(400)); // 06:40\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_contains_wrap() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"22:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert!(w.contains(1350)); // 22:30\n        assert!(w.contains(300)); // 05:00\n        assert!(!w.contains(700)); // 11:40\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_boundary_start() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert!(w.contains(120)); // exactly 02:00\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_boundary_end_exclusive() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert!(!w.contains(360)); // exactly 06:00 is excluded\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_invalid_format() {\n        assert!(TimeWindow::parse(\"not-a-window\").is_err());\n        assert!(TimeWindow::parse(\"25:00-06:00\").is_err());\n        assert!(TimeWindow::parse(\"02:60-06:00\").is_err());\n    }\n\n    // ---- Window guard ----\n\n    #[test]\n    fn window_guard_no_restriction() {\n        let config = ReshardingConfig::default();\n        assert_eq!(check_window(0, &config), WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn window_guard_allowed() {\n        let config = ReshardingConfig {\n            allowed_windows: vec![\"02:00-06:00\".into()],\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let result = check_window(180, &config); // 03:00\n        assert!(matches!(result, WindowGuardResult::Allowed { .. }));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn window_guard_denied() {\n        let config = ReshardingConfig {\n            allowed_windows: vec![\"02:00-06:00\".into()],\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let result = check_window(720, &config); // 12:00\n        assert!(matches!(result, WindowGuardResult::Denied { .. }));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn window_guard_multiple_windows() {\n        let config = ReshardingConfig {\n            allowed_windows: vec![\"02:00-04:00\".into(), \"22:00-23:30\".into()],\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        // In first window.\n        assert!(matches!(\n            check_window(150, &config),\n            WindowGuardResult::Allowed { .. }\n        ));\n        // In second window.\n        assert!(matches!(\n            check_window(1350, &config),\n            WindowGuardResult::Allowed { .. }\n        ));\n        // Outside both.\n        assert!(matches!(\n            check_window(720, &config),\n            WindowGuardResult::Denied { .. }\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // ---- Simulation ----\n\n    #[test]\n    fn simulation_storage_always_2x() {\n        // Regardless of parameters, peak storage should be exactly 2× normal.\n        let params = SimParams {\n            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n            corpus_size_bytes: 10 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024, // 10 GB\n            write_rate_dps: 100,\n            replica_groups: 2,\n            replication_factor: 1,\n            old_shards: 64,\n            new_shards: 128,\n            nodes_per_group: 3,\n            backfill_throttle_dps: 10_000,\n        };\n        let result = simulate(&params);\n        assert!(\n            (result.storage_amplification - 2.0).abs() < 0.01,\n            \"expected ~2.0, got {}\",\n            result.storage_amplification\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn simulation_dual_write_is_2x() {\n        let params = SimParams {\n            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n            corpus_size_bytes: 1024 * 1024,\n            write_rate_dps: 100,\n            replica_groups: 2,\n            replication_factor: 1,\n            old_shards: 16,\n            new_shards: 32,\n            nodes_per_group: 3,\n            backfill_throttle_dps: 1000,\n        };\n        let result = simulate(&params);\n        assert!(\n            (result.dual_write_amplification - 2.0).abs() < 0.01,\n            \"expected 2.0, got {}\",\n            result.dual_write_amplification\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn simulation_low_cv_with_many_docs() {\n        // With enough docs, hash distribution CV should be very low (< 5%).\n        let params = SimParams {\n            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n            corpus_size_bytes: 1_000_000 * 1024, // 1M docs × 1KB\n            write_rate_dps: 100,\n            replica_groups: 1,\n            replication_factor: 1,\n            old_shards: 16,\n            new_shards: 64,\n            nodes_per_group: 4,\n            backfill_throttle_dps: 1000,\n        };\n        let result = simulate(&params);\n        assert!(\n            result.old_shard_cv < 0.05,\n            \"old shard CV too high: {}\",\n            result.old_shard_cv\n        );\n        assert!(\n            result.new_shard_cv < 0.05,\n            \"new shard CV too high: {}\",\n            result.new_shard_cv\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_display_roundtrip() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:30-06:45\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(format!(\"{}\", w), \"02:30-06:45\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_parse_missing_dash() {\n        let err = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00\").unwrap_err();\n        assert!(err.contains(\"expected HH:MM-HH:MM\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_parse_invalid_hm() {\n        let err = TimeWindow::parse(\"ab:00-06:00\").unwrap_err();\n        assert!(err.contains(\"invalid hour\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_parse_invalid_minute() {\n        let err = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:ab-06:00\").unwrap_err();\n        assert!(err.contains(\"invalid minute\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn check_window_now_no_restriction() {\n        let config = ReshardingConfig::default();\n        assert!(matches!(check_window_now(&config), WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn simulation_throttle_zero_gives_infinity_duration() {\n        let params = SimParams {\n            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n            corpus_size_bytes: 1024 * 1024,\n            write_rate_dps: 100,\n            replica_groups: 1,\n            replication_factor: 1,\n            old_shards: 4,\n            new_shards: 8,\n            nodes_per_group: 2,\n            backfill_throttle_dps: 0,\n        };\n        let result = simulate(&params);\n        assert!(result.backfill_duration_secs.is_infinite());\n        assert_eq!(result.total_bytes_written, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn cv_empty_and_zero() {\n        assert_eq!(cv(&[]), 0.0);\n        assert_eq!(cv(&[0, 0, 0]), 0.0);\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Six-phase resharding execution (plan §13.1)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Resharding phase identifiers (matching plan §13.1 steps).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[repr(u8)]\npub enum ReshardPhase {\n    /// No active resharding.\n    Idle = 0,\n    /// Phase 1: Shadow index created.\n    ShadowCreated = 1,\n    /// Phase 2: Dual-hash dual-write active.\n    DualWriteActive = 2,\n    /// Phase 3: Backfill in progress.\n    BackfillInProgress = 3,\n    /// Phase 4: Verification in progress.\n    Verifying = 4,\n    /// Phase 5: Alias swap completed.\n    Swapped = 5,\n    /// Phase 6: Cleanup in progress.\n    CleaningUp = 6,\n    /// Resharding completed successfully.\n    Complete = 7,\n    /// Resharding failed.\n    Failed = 8,\n}\n\nimpl ReshardPhase {\n    /// Human-readable phase name.\n    pub fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::Idle => \"Idle\",\n            Self::ShadowCreated => \"Shadow Created\",\n            Self::DualWriteActive => \"Dual-Write Active\",\n            Self::BackfillInProgress => \"Backfill In Progress\",\n            Self::Verifying => \"Verifying\",\n            Self::Swapped => \"Swapped\",\n            Self::CleaningUp => \"Cleaning Up\",\n            Self::Complete => \"Complete\",\n            Self::Failed => \"Failed\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Parse from u8 (for storage).\n    pub fn from_u8(v: u8) -> Option<Self> {\n        match v {\n            0 => Some(Self::Idle),\n            1 => Some(Self::ShadowCreated),\n            2 => Some(Self::DualWriteActive),\n            3 => Some(Self::BackfillInProgress),\n            4 => Some(Self::Verifying),\n            5 => Some(Self::Swapped),\n            6 => Some(Self::CleaningUp),\n            7 => Some(Self::Complete),\n            8 => Some(Self::Failed),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Active resharding operation state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ReshardOperation {\n    /// Unique operation ID.\n    pub id: String,\n    /// Index UID being resharded.\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    /// Old shard count.\n    pub old_shards: u32,\n    /// New shard count.\n    pub target_shards: u32,\n    /// Current phase.\n    pub phase: ReshardPhase,\n    /// Phase started at (UNIX ms).\n    pub phase_started_at: u64,\n    /// Operation created at (UNIX ms).\n    pub created_at: u64,\n    /// Documents backfilled so far.\n    pub documents_backfilled: u64,\n    /// Total documents to backfill (estimated at start).\n    pub total_documents: u64,\n    /// Last error message (if any).\n    pub last_error: Option<String>,\n    /// Shadow index UID.\n    pub shadow_index: String,\n    /// Verification results (populated after phase 4).\n    pub verification_results: Option<VerificationResults>,\n    /// Cleanup retention deadline (UNIX ms).\n    pub cleanup_deadline: Option<u64>,\n}\n\n/// Results from phase 4 verification.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct VerificationResults {\n    /// Live index PK set size.\n    pub live_pk_count: u64,\n    /// Shadow index PK set size.\n    pub shadow_pk_count: u64,\n    /// PKs only in live index.\n    pub live_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n    /// PKs only in shadow index.\n    pub shadow_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n    /// PKs with content hash mismatch.\n    pub mismatched_pks: Vec<String>,\n    /// Whether verification passed.\n    pub passed: bool,\n}\n\nimpl ReshardOperation {\n    /// Create a new resharding operation.\n    pub fn new(index_uid: String, old_shards: u32, target_shards: u32) -> Self {\n        let id = format!(\"reshard-{}-{}\", index_uid, uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n        let shadow_index = format!(\"{}__reshard_{}\", index_uid, target_shards);\n        let now = millis_now();\n        Self {\n            id,\n            index_uid,\n            old_shards,\n            target_shards,\n            phase: ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated,\n            phase_started_at: now,\n            created_at: now,\n            documents_backfilled: 0,\n            total_documents: 0,\n            last_error: None,\n            shadow_index,\n            verification_results: None,\n            cleanup_deadline: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Transition to the next phase.\n    pub fn advance_phase(&mut self, new_phase: ReshardPhase) {\n        self.phase = new_phase;\n        self.phase_started_at = millis_now();\n    }\n\n    /// Record an error and mark as failed.\n    pub fn fail(&mut self, error: String) {\n        self.last_error = Some(error);\n        self.phase = ReshardPhase::Failed;\n    }\n\n    /// Update backfill progress.\n    pub fn update_backfill_progress(&mut self, backfilled: u64, total: u64) {\n        self.documents_backfilled = backfilled;\n        self.total_documents = total;\n    }\n\n    /// Calculate backfill progress ratio (0.0 to 1.0).\n    pub fn backfill_progress(&self) -> f64 {\n        if self.total_documents == 0 {\n            return 0.0;\n        }\n        (self.documents_backfilled as f64) / (self.total_documents as f64)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the operation is in a terminal state.\n    pub fn is_terminal(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(\n            self.phase,\n            ReshardPhase::Complete | ReshardPhase::Failed\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Get the shadow index name.\n    pub fn shadow_index_name(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.shadow_index\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\nfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as u64\n}\n\n/// In-memory registry of active resharding operations.\n///\n/// In production, this is persisted to the task store (SQLite/Redis).\n/// This in-memory version is for single-pod development.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\npub struct ReshardRegistry {\n    operations: HashMap<String, ReshardOperation>,\n    /// Index UID -> active operation ID (at most one per index).\n    index_ops: HashMap<String, String>,\n}\n\nimpl ReshardRegistry {\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new resharding operation.\n    pub fn register(&mut self, op: ReshardOperation) -> Result<(), String> {\n        if let Some(existing_id) = self.index_ops.get(&op.index_uid) {\n            return Err(format!(\n                \"Resharding already in progress for index '{}': {}\",\n                op.index_uid, existing_id\n            ));\n        }\n        self.index_ops.insert(op.index_uid.clone(), op.id.clone());\n        self.operations.insert(op.id.clone(), op);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get an operation by ID.\n    pub fn get(&self, id: &str) -> Option<&ReshardOperation> {\n        self.operations.get(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get mutable reference for updates.\n    pub fn get_mut(&mut self, id: &str) -> Option<&mut ReshardOperation> {\n        self.operations.get_mut(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the active operation for an index (if any).\n    pub fn get_for_index(&self, index_uid: &str) -> Option<&ReshardOperation> {\n        self.index_ops\n            .get(index_uid)\n            .and_then(|id| self.operations.get(id))\n    }\n\n    /// Update an operation.\n    pub fn update(&mut self, op: ReshardOperation) -> Result<(), String> {\n        if !self.operations.contains_key(&op.id) {\n            return Err(format!(\"Operation '{}' not found\", op.id));\n        }\n        self.operations.insert(op.id.clone(), op);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Complete an operation and remove from active index tracking.\n    pub fn complete(&mut self, id: &str) -> Result<(), String> {\n        let op = self.operations.get(id).ok_or_else(|| format!(\"Operation '{}' not found\", id))?;\n        if !op.is_terminal() {\n            return Err(format!(\"Operation '{}' is not in a terminal state\", id));\n        }\n        self.index_ops.remove(&op.index_uid);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// List all operations.\n    pub fn list(&self) -> Vec<&ReshardOperation> {\n        self.operations.values().collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up completed operations older than the retention period.\n    pub fn prune_completed(&mut self, max_age_ms: u64) {\n        let now = millis_now();\n        let mut to_remove = Vec::new();\n        for (id, op) in &self.operations {\n            if op.is_terminal() && (now.saturating_sub(op.created_at) > max_age_ms) {\n                to_remove.push(id.clone());\n                self.index_ops.remove(&op.index_uid);\n            }\n        }\n        for id in to_remove {\n            self.operations.remove(&id);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests_reshard_execution {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn phase_display_names() {\n        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::Idle.name(), \"Idle\");\n        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::BackfillInProgress.name(), \"Backfill In Progress\");\n        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::Failed.name(), \"Failed\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn phase_roundtrip_u8() {\n        for phase in &[\n            ReshardPhase::Idle,\n            ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated,\n            ReshardPhase::DualWriteActive,\n            ReshardPhase::BackfillInProgress,\n            ReshardPhase::Verifying,\n            ReshardPhase::Swapped,\n            ReshardPhase::CleaningUp,\n            ReshardPhase::Complete,\n            ReshardPhase::Failed,\n        ] {\n            let v = *phase as u8;\n            assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::from_u8(v), Some(*phase));\n        }\n        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::from_u8(255), None);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn operation_creation() {\n        let op = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 64, 128);\n        assert_eq!(op.index_uid, \"products\");\n        assert_eq!(op.old_shards, 64);\n        assert_eq!(op.target_shards, 128);\n        assert_eq!(op.shadow_index, \"products__reshard_128\");\n        assert_eq!(op.phase, ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated);\n        assert!(!op.is_terminal());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn operation_backfill_progress() {\n        let mut op = ReshardOperation::new(\"test\".into(), 16, 32);\n        op.total_documents = 1000;\n        op.documents_backfilled = 500;\n        assert!((op.backfill_progress() - 0.5).abs() < f64::EPSILON);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn operation_terminal_states() {\n        let mut op = ReshardOperation::new(\"test\".into(), 16, 32);\n        assert!(!op.is_terminal());\n        op.phase = ReshardPhase::Complete;\n        assert!(op.is_terminal());\n        op.phase = ReshardPhase::Failed;\n        assert!(op.is_terminal());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_single_op_per_index() {\n        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n        let op1 = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 64, 128);\n        reg.register(op1).unwrap();\n        let op2 = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 128, 256);\n        assert!(reg.register(op2).is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_get_for_index() {\n        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n        let op = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 64, 128);\n        let id = op.id.clone();\n        reg.register(op).unwrap();\n        let retrieved = reg.get_for_index(\"products\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.id, id);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_complete_releases_index() {\n        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n        let op = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 64, 128);\n        let id = op.id.clone();\n        reg.register(op).unwrap();\n        assert!(reg.get_for_index(\"products\").is_some());\n        let mut op = reg.get_mut(&id).unwrap();\n        op.phase = ReshardPhase::Complete;\n        reg.complete(&id).unwrap();\n        assert!(reg.get_for_index(\"products\").is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_prune_old_completed() {\n        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n        let mut op = ReshardOperation::new(\"test\".into(), 16, 32);\n        op.phase = ReshardPhase::Complete;\n        op.created_at = millis_now().saturating_sub(100_000); // 100s ago\n        let id = op.id.clone();\n        reg.register(op).unwrap();\n        reg.prune_completed(50_000); // prune ops older than 50s\n        assert!(reg.get(&id).is_none());\n    }\n}\n","numLines":1022,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1022}}}

serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Current phase of a settings broadcast.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[repr(u8)]\npub enum BroadcastPhase {\n    Idle = 0,\n    Propose = 1,\n    Verify = 2,\n    Commit = 3,\n}\n\n/// Status of an ongoing settings self.node_settings_version.read().await;\n        *versions.get(&(index.to_string(), node_id.to_string())).unwrap_or(&0)\n    }\n\n    /// Start a new settings broadcast (Phase 1: Propose).\n    ///\n Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n24\tpub struct Alias {\n25\t    /// Alias name (the key clients use).\n26\t    pub name: String,\n27\t    /// `single` or `multi`.\n28\t    pub kind: AliasKind,\n29\t Option<Vec<String>>,\n33\t    /// Generation incremented on each flip.\n34\t    pub generation: u64,\n35\t    /// Created at timestamp.\n36\t    pub created_at: u64,\n37\t    /// Last updated timestamp.\n38\t    pub updated_at: u64,\n39\t}\n40\t\n41\timpl Alias {\n42\t    /// Create a new single-target alias.\n43\t    pub fn new_single(name: String, target_uid: String) -> Self {\n44\t name,\n50\t            kind: AliasKind::Single,\n51\t            current_uid: Some(target_uid),\n52\t            target_uids: None,\n53\t            generation: 0,\n54\t            created_at: now,\n55\t            updated_at: now,\n56\t        }\n57\t    }\n58\t\n59\t    /// Create a new multi-target alias.\n60\t    pub fn new_multi(name: String, target_uids: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n61\t match self.kind {\n79\t            AliasKind::Single => {\n80\t                let uid = self.current_uid.as_ref()\n81\t                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"single alias missing current_uid\".into()))?;\n82\t                Ok(vec![uid.clone()])\n83\t            }\n84\t            AliasKind::Multi => {\n85\t                let uids = self.target_uids.as_ref()\n86\t                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"multi alias missing target_uids\".into()))?;\n87\t                Ok(uids.clone())\n88\t            }\n89\t        }\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t Atomic index aliases for blue-green reindexing (plan §13.7).\n//!\n//! This module implements the alias layer that allows atomic index flips\n//! without downtime. Aliases resolve to one or more concrete Meilisearch\n//! index UIDs, supporting both single-target (writable) and multi-target\n//! (read-only, used by ILM) aliases.\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Alias kind: single-target (writable) or multi-target (read-only, ILM-managed).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"lowercase\")]\npub enum AliasKind {\n    Single,\n    Multi,\n}\n\n/// A single alias record from the task store.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Alias {\n    /// Alias name (the key clients use).\n    pub name: String,\n    /// `single` or `multi`.\n    pub kind: AliasKind,\n    /// Current target UID (only set when kind=single).\n    pub current_uid: Option<String>,\n    /// Target UIDs as JSON array (only set when kind=multi).\n    pub target_uids: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    /// Generation incremented on each flip.\n    pub generation: u64,\n    /// Created at timestamp.\n    pub created_at: u64,\n    /// Last updated timestamp.\n    pub updated_at: u64,\n}\n\nimpl Alias {\n    /// Create a new single-target alias.\n    pub fn new_single(name: String, target_uid: String) -> Self {\n        let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        Self {\n            name,\n            kind: AliasKind::Single,\n            current_uid: Some(target_uid),\n            target_uids: None,\n            generation: 0,\n            created_at: now,\n            updated_at: now,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new multi-target alias.\n    pub fn new_multi(name: String, target_uids: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n        let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        Self {\n            name,\n            kind: AliasKind::Multi,\n            current_uid: None,\n            target_uids: Some(target_uids),\n            generation: 0,\n            created_at: now,\n            updated_at: now,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the effective target UIDs for this alias.\n    pub fn targets(&self) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n        match self.kind {\n            AliasKind::Single => {\n                let uid = self.current_uid.as_ref()\n                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"single alias missing current_uid\".into()))?;\n                Ok(vec![uid.clone()])\n            }\n            AliasKind::Multi => {\n                let uids = self.target_uids.as_ref()\n                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"multi alias missing target_uids\".into()))?;\n                Ok(uids.clone())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Flip this alias to a new target (single-target only).\n    pub fn flip(&mut self, new_target: String) -> Result<()> {\n        if self.kind != AliasKind::Single {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"cannot flip multi-target alias\".into()));\n        }\n        self.current_uid = Some(new_target);\n        self.generation += 1;\n        self.updated_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":289}}}

Anti-entropy reconciler module.\n//!\n//! Stub for plan §13.8 anti-entropy shard reconciler.\n//! Full implementation will follow the fingerprint → diff → repair pipeline.\n\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n\nuse crate::migration::{MigrationConfig, MigrationError};\n\n/// Anti-entropy configuration (plan §13.8).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct AntiEntropyConfig {\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    pub schedule_cron: String,\n    pub shards_per_pass: u32,\n    pub max_read_concurrency: u32,\n    pub fingerprint_batch_size: u32,\n    pub auto_repair: bool,\n    pub updated_at_field: String,\n}\n\nimpl Default for AntiEntropyConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            schedule_cron: \"0 */6 * * *\".to_string(),\n            shards_per_pass: 0,\n            max_read_concurrency: 2,\n            fingerprint_batch_size: 1000,\n            auto_repair: true,\n            updated_at_field: \"_miroir_updated_at\".to_string(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Validates that migration is safe given the anti-entropy configuration.\n/// Returns Ok(()) if safe, Err with a descriptive message if not.\n///\n/// Hard refusal policy (plan §15 OP#1): skipping the delta pass while\n/// anti-entropy is disabled provides zero recovery path for documents\n/// written at the cutover boundary. Measured loss rate: ~2% of writes.\n/// This is a hard-coded policy, not a warning.\npub fn validate_migration_safety(\n    ae_config: &AntiEntropyConfig,\n    migration_config: &MigrationConfig,\n) -> Result<(), MigrationError> {\n    if migration_config.skip_delta_pass && !ae_config.enabled {\n        return Err(MigrationError::UnsafeCutoverNoAntiEntropy);\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Generates a warning if anti-entropy is disabled during active migration.\n/// The caller should log this at warn level.\n///\n/// Even with the delta pass enabled (which provides 0-loss cutover on its own),\n/// disabling anti-entropy means the delta pass is the sole safety mechanism.\n/// Operators should be aware of this reduced redundancy.\npub fn migration_warning_if_ae_disabled(ae_enabled: bool) -> Option<String> {\n    if ae_enabled {\n        return None;\n    }\n    Some(\n        \"Anti-entropy is disabled. Shard migration cutover relies on the delta pass \\\n         as the sole safety mechanism. Any bugs in the delta pass could lead to \\\n         data loss at the cutover boundary. Re-enable anti-entropy for defense-in-depth.\"\n            .to_string(),\n    )\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_safe_with_delta_pass() {\n        let ae = AntiEntropyConfig {\n            enabled: false,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let mc = MigrationConfig {\n            skip_delta_pass: false,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        assert!(validate_migration_safety(&ae, &mc).is_ok());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_unsafe_without_anti_entropy() {\n        let ae = AntiEntropyConfig {\n            enabled: false,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let mc = MigrationConfig {\n            skip_delta_pass: true,\n            anti_entropy_enabled: false,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        assert!(validate_migration_safety(&ae, &mc).is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_safe_with_anti_entropy_safety_net() {\n        let ae = AntiEntropyConfig {\n            enabled: true,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let mc = MigrationConfig {\n            skip_delta_pass: true,\n            anti_entropy_enabled: true,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        assert!(validate_migration_safety(&ae, &mc).is_ok());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_warning_when_ae_disabled() {\n        assert!(migration_warning_if_ae_disabled(false).is_some());\n        assert!(migration_warning_if_ae_disabled(true).is_none());\n    }\n}\n","numLines":122,"startLine":1,"totalLines":122}}}

ILM (Index Lifecycle Management) — plan §13.17.\n//!\n//! Manages rolling time-series indexes with automatic rollover and retention.\n\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse tracing::{info, warn, error};\n\n/// ILM rollover policy configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RolloverPolicy {\n    /// Policy name.\n    pub name: String,\n    /// Write alias name.\n    pub write_alias: String,\n    /// Read alias name (multi-target).\n    pub read_alias: String,\n    /// Index name pattern with {YYYY-MM-DD} placeholder.\n    pub pattern: String,\n    /// Rollover triggers.\n    pub triggers: RolloverTriggers,\n    /// Retention policy.\n    pub retention: RetentionPolicy,\n    /// Index template reference.\n    pub index_template: IndexTemplate,\n}\n\n/// Triggers that cause a rollover.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RolloverTriggers {\n    /// Maximum documents before rollover.\n    pub max_docs: u64,\n    /// Maximum age before rollover (e.g., \"7d\").\n    pub max_age: String,\n    /// Maximum index size before rollover (GB).\n    pub max_size_gb: u32,\n}\n\n/// Retention policy for old indexes.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RetentionPolicy {\n    /// Number of indexes to keep.\n    pub keep_indexes: u32,\n}\n\n/// Index template for rollover.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct IndexTemplate {\n    /// Primary key field.\n    pub primary_key: String,\n    /// Named settings profile reference.\n    pub settings_ref: String,\n}\n\n/// ILM manager state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct IlmState {\n    /// Registered policies.\n    pub policies: Vec<RolloverPolicy>,\n    /// Active rollover operations.\n    pub active_rollovers: HashMap<String, RolloverOperation>,\n    /// Last check timestamp (UNIX ms).\n    pub last_check_ms: u64,\n}\n\n/// Active rollover operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RolloverOperation {\n    /// Policy name.\n    pub policy_name: String,\n    /// Current phase.\n    pub phase: RolloverPhase,\n    /// New index UID.\n    pub new_index: String,\n    /// Old index UID.\n    pub old_index: String,\n    /// Started at (UNIX ms).\n    pub started_at: u64,\n    /// Error message if failed.\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Rollover phase.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum RolloverPhase {\n    Creating,\n    FlippingAlias,\n    UpdatingReadAlias,\n    CleaningOld,\n    Complete,\n    Failed,\n}\n\n/// ILM manager — handles index lifecycle for time-series data.\npub struct IlmManager {\n    /// Configuration.\n    config: IlmConfig,\n    /// Shared state.\n    state: Arc<RwLock<IlmState>>,\n}\n\n/// ILM manager configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct IlmConfig {\n    /// Whether ILM is enabled.\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    /// Check interval (seconds).\n    pub check_interval_s: u64,\n    /// Safety lock: refuse to delete indexes newer than this (days).\n    pub safety_lock_older_than_days: u32,\n    /// Maximum rollovers per check.\n    pub max_rollovers_per_check: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Default for IlmConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            check_interval_s: 3600,\n            safety_lock_older_than_days: 7,\n            max_rollovers_per_check: 10,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl IlmManager {\n    /// Create a new ILM manager.\n    pub fn new(config: IlmConfig) -> Self {\n        let state = Arc::new(RwLock::new(IlmState {\n            policies: Vec::new(),\n            active_rollovers: HashMap::new(),\n            last_check_ms: 0,\n        }));\n\n        if config.enabled {\n            // Spawn background evaluator\n            let state_clone = state.clone();\n            let config_clone = config.clone();\n            tokio::spawn(async move {\n                Self::background_evaluator(state_clone, config_clone).await;\n            });\n        }\n\n        Self { config, state }\n    }\n\n    /// Register a rollover policy.\n    pub async fn register_policy(&self, policy: RolloverPolicy) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        let mut state = self.state.write().await;\n        state.policies.push(policy);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Unregister a policy.\n    pub async fn unregister_policy(&self, name: &str) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        let mut state = self.state.write().await;\n        state.policies.retain(|p| p.name != name);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get all policies.\n    pub async fn policies(&self) -> Vec<RolloverPolicy> {\n        let state = self.state.read().await;\n        state.policies.clone()\n    }\n\n    /// Get active rollover for a policy.\n    pub async fn active_rollover(&self, policy_name: &str) -> Option<RolloverOperation> {\n        let state = self.state.read().await;\n        state.active_rollovers.get(policy_name).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Trigger an immediate rollover for a policy.\n    pub async fn trigger_rollover(&self, policy_name: &str) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        let state = self.state.read().await;\n        let policy = state.policies.iter()\n            .find(|p| p.name == policy_name)\n            .ok_or_else(|| IlmError::PolicyNotFound(policy_name.to_string()))?;\n\n        // Create rollover operation\n        let now = millis_now();\n        let new_index = Self::format_index_name(&policy.pattern, now);\n        let operation = RolloverOperation {\n            policy_name: policy_name.to_string(),\n            phase: RolloverPhase::Creating,\n            new_index: new_index.clone(),\n            old_index: format!(\"{}-current\", policy.write_alias),\n            started_at: now,\n            error: None,\n        };\n\n        drop(state);\n        let mut state = self.state.write().await;\n        state.active_rollovers.insert(policy_name.to_string(), operation);\n\n        info!(\"ILM: triggered rollover for policy '{}', new index: {}\", policy_name, new_index);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Background evaluator that checks policies and performs rollovers.\n    async fn background_evaluator(\n        state: Arc<RwLock<IlmState>>,\n        config: IlmConfig,\n    ) {\n        info!(\"ILM: background evaluator started\");\n\n        let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(tokio::time::Duration::from_secs(config.check_interval_s));\n        loop {\n            interval.tick().await;\n\n            let policies = {\n                let state = state.read().await;\n                state.policies.clone()\n            };\n\n            for policy in policies.iter().take(config.max_rollovers_per_check as usize) {\n                if let Err(e) = Self::evaluate_policy(&state, &policy, &config).await {\n                    error!(\"ILM: error evaluating policy '{}': {}\", policy.name, e);\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Update last check time\n            {\n                let mut state = state.write().await;\n                state.last_check_ms = millis_now();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Evaluate a single policy and perform rollover if needed.\n    async fn evaluate_policy(\n        state: &Arc<RwLock<IlmState>>,\n        policy: &RolloverPolicy,\n        _config: &IlmConfig,\n    ) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        // Check if there's already an active rollover\n        {\n            let state = state.read().await;\n            if state.active_rollovers.contains_key(&policy.name) {\n                return Ok(()); // Skip if rollover in progress\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check triggers (placeholder - would query actual stats in production)\n        let should_rollover = false; // TODO: implement trigger checking\n\n        if should_rollover {\n            // Trigger rollover\n            let now = millis_now();\n            let new_index = Self::format_index_name(&policy.pattern, now);\n            let operation = RolloverOperation {\n                policy_name: policy.name.clone(),\n                phase: RolloverPhase::Creating,\n                new_index,\n                old_index: format!(\"{}-current\", policy.write_alias),\n                started_at: now,\n                error: None,\n            };\n\n            let mut state = state.write().await;\n            state.active_rollovers.insert(policy.name.clone(), operation);\n\n            info!(\"ILM: auto-triggered rollover for policy '{}'\", policy.name);\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Format index name from pattern with date placeholder.\n    fn format_index_name(pattern: &str, timestamp_ms: u64) -> String {\n        // Convert milliseconds to seconds since epoch\n        let timestamp_sec = (timestamp_ms / 1000) as i64;\n\n        // Manual calculation of date from Unix timestamp\n        // This is accurate for dates from 1970 to 2100+\n        let days_since_epoch = timestamp_sec / 86400;\n\n        // Algorithm to convert days to year/month/day\n        // Based on: https://howardhinnant.github.io/date_algorithms.html\n        let mut era = (days_since_epoch >= 0 ? days_since_epoch : days_since_epoch - 146096 + 1) / 146097;\n        let mut doe = days_since_epoch - era * 146097;\n        let mut yoe = (doe - doe / 1460 + doe / 36524 - doe / 146096) / 365;\n        let mut doy = doe - (365 * yoe + yoe / 4 - yoe / 100);\n        let mut mp = (5 * doy + 2) / 153;\n        let mut d = doy - (153 * mp + 2) / 5 + 1;\n        let mut m = mp + (mp < 10 ? 3 : -9);\n        let mut y = yoe + era * 400;\n\n        if m <= 2 {\n            y -= 1;\n            m += 12;\n        }\n\n        let date_str = format!(\"{:04}-{:02}-{:02}\", y, m, d);\n        pattern.replace(\"{YYYY-MM-DD}\", &date_str)\n    }\n}\n\n/// ILM error types.\n#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]\npub enum IlmError {\n    #[error(\"policy not found: {0}\")]\n    PolicyNotFound(String),\n    #[error(\"rollover failed: {0}\")]\n    RolloverFailed(String),\n    #[error(\"alias error: {0}\")]\n    AliasError(String),\n    #[error(\"safety lock violation: index is too new to delete\")]\n    SafetyLockViolation,\n}\n\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\nfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n    use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as u64\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_ilm_config_default() {\n        let config = IlmConfig::default();\n        assert!(config.enabled);\n        assert_eq!(config.check_interval_s, 3600);\n        assert_eq!(config.safety_lock_older_than_days, 7);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_format_index_name() {\n        let pattern = \"logs-{YYYY-MM-DD}\";\n        let timestamp = 1704067200000; // 2024-01-01 00:00:00 UTC\n        let result = IlmManager::format_index_name(pattern, timestamp);\n        assert_eq!(result, \"logs-2024-01-01\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rollover_phase_serialization() {\n        let phase = RolloverPhase::Creating;\n        let json = serde_json::to_string(&phase).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(json, \"\\\"Creating\\\"\");\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_register_policy() {\n        let config = IlmConfig::default();\n        let manager = IlmManager::new(config);\n\n        let policy = RolloverPolicy {\n {\n                keep_indexes: 30,\n            },\n            index_template: IndexTemplate {\n                primary_key: \"event_id\".into(),\n                settings_ref: \"logs-settings\".into(),\n            },\n        };\n\n        assert!(manager.register_policy(policy).await.is_ok());\n        let policies = manager.policies().await;\n        assert_eq!(policies.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(policies[0].name, \"logs-ilm\");\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_unregister_policy() {\n        let config = IlmConfig::default();\n        let manager = IlmManager::new(config);\n\n        let policy = RolloverPolicy {\n            name: \"test-policy\".into(),\n            write_alias: \"test\".into(),\n            read_alias: \"test-search\".into(),\n            pattern: \"test-{YYYY-MM-DD}\".into(),\n            triggers: RolloverTriggers {\n                max_docs: 1000,\n                max_age: \"1d\".into(),\n                max_size_gb: 10,\n            },\n            retention: RetentionPolicy {\n                keep_indexes: 7,\n            },\n            index_template: IndexTemplate {\n                primary_key: \"id\".into(),\n                settings_ref: \"default\".into(),\n            },\n        };\n\n        manager.register_policy(policy).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(manager.policies().await.len(), 1);\n\n        manager.unregister_policy(\"test-policy\").await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(manager.policies().await.len(), 0);\n    }\n\n name: \"test-rollover\".into(),\n            write_alias: \"logs\".into(),\n            read_alias: \"logs-search\".into(),\n            pattern: \"logs-{YYYY-MM-DD}\".into(),\n            triggers: ILM (Index Lifecycle Management) — plan §13.17.\n//!\n//! Manages rolling time-series indexes with automatic rollover and retention.\n\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse tracing::{info, warn, error};\n\n/// ILM rollover policy configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RolloverPolicy {\n    /// Policy name.\n    pub name: String,\n    /// Write alias name.\n    pub write_alias: String,\n    /// Read alias name (multi-target).\n    pub read_alias: String,\n    /// Index name pattern with {YYYY-MM-DD} placeholder.\n    pub pattern: String,\n    /// Rollover triggers.\n    pub triggers: RolloverTriggers,\n    /// Retention policy.\n    pub retention: RetentionPolicy,\n    /// Index template reference.\n    pub index_template: IndexTemplate,\n}\n\n/// Triggers that cause a rollover.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RolloverTriggers {\n    /// Maximum documents before rollover.\n    pub max_docs: u64,\n    /// Maximum age before rollover (e.g., \"7d\").\n    pub max_age: String,\n    /// Maximum index size before rollover (GB).\n    pub max_size_gb: u32,\n}\n\n/// Retention policy for old indexes.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RetentionPolicy {\n    /// Number of indexes to keep.\n    pub keep_indexes: u32,\n}\n\n/// Index template for rollover.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct IndexTemplate {\n    /// Primary key field.\n    pub primary_key: String,\n    /// Named settings profile reference.\n    pub settings_ref: String,\n}\n\n/// ILM manager state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct IlmState {\n    /// Registered policies.\n    pub policies: Vec<RolloverPolicy>,\n    /// Active rollover operations.\n    pub active_rollovers: HashMap<String, RolloverOperation>,\n    /// Last check timestamp (UNIX ms).\n    pub last_check_ms: u64,\n}\n\n/// Active rollover operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RolloverOperation {\n    /// Policy name.\n    pub policy_name: String,\n    /// Current phase.\n    pub phase: RolloverPhase,\n    /// New index UID.\n    pub new_index: String,\n    /// Old index UID.\n    pub old_index: String,\n    /// Started at (UNIX ms).\n    pub started_at: u64,\n    /// Error message if failed.\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Rollover phase.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum RolloverPhase {\n    Creating,\n    FlippingAlias,\n    UpdatingReadAlias,\n    CleaningOld,\n    Complete,\n    Failed,\n}\n\n/// ILM manager — handles index lifecycle for time-series data.\npub struct IlmManager {\n    /// Configuration.\n    config: IlmConfig,\n    /// Shared state.\n    state: Arc<RwLock<IlmState>>,\n}\n\n/// ILM manager configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct IlmConfig {\n    /// Whether ILM is enabled.\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    /// Check interval (seconds).\n    pub check_interval_s: u64,\n    /// Safety lock: refuse to delete indexes newer than this (days).\n    pub safety_lock_older_than_days: u32,\n    /// Maximum rollovers per check.\n    pub max_rollovers_per_check: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Default for IlmConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            check_interval_s: 3600,\n            safety_lock_older_than_days: 7,\n            max_rollovers_per_check: 10,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl IlmManager {\n    /// Create a new ILM manager.\n    pub fn new(config: IlmConfig) -> Self {\n        let state = Arc::new(RwLock::new(IlmState {\n            policies: Vec::new(),\n            active_rollovers: HashMap::new(),\n            last_check_ms: 0,\n        }));\n\n        if config.enabled {\n            // Spawn background evaluator\n            let state_clone = state.clone();\n            let config_clone = config.clone();\n            tokio::spawn(async move {\n                Self::background_evaluator(state_clone, config_clone).await;\n            });\n        }\n\n        Self { config, state }\n    }\n\n    /// Register a rollover policy.\n    pub async fn register_policy(&self, policy: RolloverPolicy) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        let mut state = self.state.write().await;\n        state.policies.push(policy);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Unregister a policy.\n    pub async fn unregister_policy(&self, name: &str) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        let mut state = self.state.write().await;\n        state.policies.retain(|p| p.name != name);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get all policies.\n    pub async fn policies(&self) -> Vec<RolloverPolicy> {\n        let state = self.state.read().await;\n        state.policies.clone()\n    }\n\n    /// Get active rollover for a policy.\n    pub async fn active_rollover(&self, policy_name: &str) -> Option<RolloverOperation> {\n        let state = self.state.read().await;\n        state.active_rollovers.get(policy_name).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Trigger an immediate rollover for a policy.\n    pub async fn trigger_rollover(&self, policy_name: &str) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        let state = self.state.read().await;\n        let policy = state.policies.iter()\n            .find(|p| p.name == policy_name)\n            .ok_or_else(|| IlmError::PolicyNotFound(policy_name.to_string()))?;\n\n        // Create rollover operation\n        let now = millis_now();\n        let new_index = Self::format_index_name(&policy.pattern, now);\n        let operation = RolloverOperation {\n            policy_name: policy_name.to_string(),\n            phase: RolloverPhase::Creating,\n            new_index: new_index.clone(),\n            old_index: format!(\"{}-current\", policy.write_alias),\n            started_at: now,\n            error: None,\n        };\n\n        drop(state);\n        let mut state = self.state.write().await;\n        state.active_rollovers.insert(policy_name.to_string(), operation);\n\n        info!(\"ILM: triggered rollover for policy '{}', new index: {}\", policy_name, new_index);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Background evaluator that checks policies and performs rollovers.\n    async fn background_evaluator(\n        state: Arc<RwLock<IlmState>>,\n        config: IlmConfig,\n    ) {\n        info!(\"ILM: background evaluator started\");\n\n        let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(tokio::time::Duration::from_secs(config.check_interval_s));\n        loop {\n            interval.tick().await;\n\n            let policies = {\n                let state = state.read().await;\n                state.policies.clone()\n            };\n\n            for policy in policies.iter().take(config.max_rollovers_per_check as usize) {\n                if let Err(e) = Self::evaluate_policy(&state, &policy, &config).await {\n                    error!(\"ILM: error evaluating policy '{}': {}\", policy.name, e);\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Update last check time\n            {\n                let mut state = state.write().await;\n                state.last_check_ms = millis_now();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Evaluate a single policy and perform rollover if needed.\n    async fn evaluate_policy(\n        state: &Arc<RwLock<IlmState>>,\n        policy: &RolloverPolicy,\n        _config: &IlmConfig,\n    ) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        // Check if there's already an active rollover\n        {\n            let state = state.read().await;\n            if state.active_rollovers.contains_key(&policy.name) {\n                return Ok(()); // Skip if rollover in progress\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check triggers (placeholder - would query actual stats in production)\n        let should_rollover = false; // TODO: implement trigger checking\n\n        if should_rollover {\n            // Trigger rollover\n            let now = millis_now();\n            let new_index = Self::format_index_name(&policy.pattern, now);\n            let operation = RolloverOperation {\n                policy_name: policy.name.clone(),\n                phase: RolloverPhase::Creating,\n                new_index,\n                old_index: format!(\"{}-current\", policy.write_alias),\n                started_at: now,\n                error: None,\n            };\n\n            let mut state = state.write().await;\n            state.active_rollovers.insert(policy.name.clone(), operation);\n\n            info!(\"ILM: auto-triggered rollover for policy '{}'\", policy.name);\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Format index name from pattern with date placeholder.\n    fn format_index_name(pattern: &str, timestamp_ms: u64) -> String {\n        // Convert milliseconds to seconds since epoch\n        let timestamp_sec = (timestamp_ms / 1000) as i64;\n\n        // Manual calculation of date from Unix timestamp\n        // This is accurate for dates from 1970 to 2100+\n        let days_since_epoch = timestamp_sec / 86400;\n\n        // Algorithm to convert days to year/month/day\n        // Based on: https://howardhinnant.github.io/date_algorithms.html\n        let era_adjust = if days_since_epoch >= 0 {\n            days_since_epoch\n        } else {\n            days_since_epoch - 146096 + 1\n        };\n        let mut era = era_adjust / 146097;\n        let mut doe = days_since_epoch - era * 146097;\n        let mut yoe = (doe - doe / 1460 + doe / 36524 - doe / 146096) / 365;\n        let mut doy = doe - (365 * yoe + yoe / 4 - yoe / 100);\n        let mut mp = (5 * doy + 2) / 153;\n        let mut d = doy - (153 * mp + 2) / 5 + 1;\n        let m_adjust = if mp < 10 { 3 } else { -9 };\n        let mut m = mp + m_adjust;\n        let mut y = yoe + era * 400;\n\n        if m <= 2 {\n            y -= 1;\n            m += 12;\n        }\n\n        let date_str = format!(\"{:04}-{:02}-{:02}\", y, m, d);\n        pattern.replace(\"{YYYY-MM-DD}\", &date_str)\n    }\n}\n\n/// ILM error types.\n#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]\npub enum IlmError {\n    #[error(\"policy not found: {0}\")]\n    PolicyNotFound(String),\n    #[error(\"rollover failed: {0}\")]\n    RolloverFailed(String),\n    #[error(\"alias error: {0}\")]\n    AliasError(String),\n    #[error(\"safety lock violation: index is too new to delete\")]\n    SafetyLockViolation,\n}\n\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\nfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n    use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as u64\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_ilm_config_default() {\n        let config = IlmConfig::default();\n        assert!(config.enabled);\n        assert_eq!(config.check_interval_s, 3600);\n        assert_eq!(config.safety_lock_older_than_days, 7);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_format_index_name() {\n        let pattern = \"logs-{YYYY-MM-DD}\";\n        let timestamp = 1704067200000; // 2024-01-01 00:00:00 UTC\n        let result = IlmManager::format_index_name(pattern, timestamp);\n        assert_eq!(result, \"logs-2024-01-01\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rollover_phase_serialization() {\n        let phase =                max_age: \"7d\".into(),\n                max_size_gb: 50,\n            },\n            retention: RetentionPolicy {\n                keep_indexes: 30,\n            },\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_unregister_policy() {\n        let config = IlmConfig::default();\n        let manager = IlmManager::new(config);\n\n        let policy = RolloverPolicy {\n            name: \"test-policy\".into(),\n            write_alias: \"test\".into(),\n            read_alias: \"test-search\".into(),\n            pattern: \"test-{YYYY-MM-DD}\".into(),\n            triggers: RolloverTriggers {\n                max_docs: 1000,\n                max_age: \"1d\".into(),\n                max_size_gb: 10,\n            },\n            retention: RetentionPolicy {\n                keep_indexes: 7,\n            },\n            index_template: IndexTemplate {\n                primary_key: \"id\".into(),\n                settings_ref: \"default\".into(),\n
